Robotics Daily Papers

Daily papers related to Robotics, Reinforcement Learning, Vision-Language Models, World Models, LLMs, VLA, and VLN

March 11, 2026

BEACON: Language-Conditioned Navigation Affordance Prediction under Occlusion

Abstract (English): Language-conditioned local navigation requires a robot to infer a nearby traversable target location from its current observation and an open-vocabulary, relational instruction. Existing vision-language spatial grounding methods usually rely on vision-language models (VLMs) to reason in image space, producing 2D predictions tied to visible pixels. As a result, they struggle to infer target locations in occluded regions, typically caused by furniture or moving humans. To address this issue, we propose BEACON, which predicts an ego-centric Bird's-Eye View (BEV) affordance heatmap over a bounded local region including occluded areas. Given an instruction and surround-view RGB-D observations from four directions around the robot, BEACON predicts the BEV heatmap by injecting spatial cues into a VLM and fusing the VLM's output with depth-derived BEV features. Using an occlusion-aware dataset built in the Habitat simulator, we conduct detailed experimental analysis to validate both our BEV space formulation and the design choices of each module. Our method improves the accuracy averaged across geodesic thresholds by 22.74 percentage points over the state-of-the-art image-space baseline on the validation subset with occluded target locations. Our project page is: https://xin-yu-gao.github.io/beacon.

摘要（中文）: 语言条件下的局部导航要求机器人从当前观测和开放词汇、关系型指令中推断出附近可通行的目标位置。现有的视觉-语言空间定位方法通常依赖于视觉-语言模型 (VLMs) 在图像空间中进行推理，产生绑定到可见像素的 2D 预测。因此，它们难以推断被遮挡区域的目标位置，这些区域通常由家具或移动的人造成。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 BEACON，它可以预测以自我为中心的鸟瞰图 (BEV) 可行性热图，该热图覆盖包括遮挡区域在内的有界局部区域。给定指令和来自机器人周围四个方向的环视 RGB-D 观测，BEACON 通过将空间线索注入 VLM 并将 VLM 的输出与深度导出的 BEV 特征融合来预测 BEV 热图。我们使用在 Habitat 模拟器中构建的具有遮挡感知的数据集进行详细的实验分析，以验证我们的 BEV 空间公式和每个模块的设计选择。在具有遮挡目标位置的验证子集上，我们的方法在测地线阈值平均精度方面比最先进的图像空间基线提高了 22.74 个百分点。我们的项目页面是：https://xin-yu-gao.github.io/beacon.

TLDR: The paper introduces BEACON, a method for language-conditioned navigation that predicts a Bird's-Eye View affordance heatmap to infer target locations in occluded environments, outperforming image-space baselines.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了BEACON，一种语言条件导航方法，通过预测鸟瞰图的可负担性热图来推断遮挡环境中的目标位置，优于图像空间基线。

Authors: Xinyu Gao, Gang Chen, Javier Alonso-Mora

Beyond Short-Horizon: VQ-Memory for Robust Long-Horizon Manipulation in Non-Markovian Simulation Benchmarks

Abstract (English): The high cost of collecting real-robot data has made robotic simulation a scalable platform for both evaluation and data generation. Yet most existing benchmarks concentrate on simple manipulation tasks such as pick-and-place, failing to capture the non-Markovian characteristics of real-world tasks and the complexity of articulated object interactions. To address this limitation, we present RuleSafe, a new articulated manipulation benchmark built upon a scalable LLM-aided simulation framework. RuleSafe features safes with diverse unlocking mechanisms, such as key locks, password locks, and logic locks, which require different multi-stage reasoning and manipulation strategies. These LLM-generated rules produce non-Markovian and long-horizon tasks that require temporal modeling and memory-based reasoning. We further propose VQ-Memory, a compact and structured temporal representation that uses vector-quantized variational autoencoders (VQ-VAEs) to encode past proprioceptive states into discrete latent tokens. This representation filters low-level noise while preserving high-level task-phase context, providing lightweight yet robust temporal cues that are compatible with existing Vision-Language-Action models (VLA). Extensive experiments on state-of-the-art VLA models and diffusion policies show that VQ-Memory consistently improves long-horizon planning, enhances generalization to unseen configurations, and enables more efficient manipulation with reduced computational cost. Project page: vqmemory.github.io

摘要（中文）: 收集真实机器人数据的高昂成本使得机器人仿真成为评估和数据生成的可扩展平台。然而，现有的大多数基准测试都集中在简单的操作任务上，例如抓取和放置，未能捕捉到真实世界任务的非马尔可夫特性以及铰接物体交互的复杂性。为了解决这一局限性，我们提出了RuleSafe，一个基于可扩展的LLM辅助仿真框架构建的新的铰接操作基准测试。RuleSafe包含具有各种解锁机制的保险箱，例如钥匙锁、密码锁和逻辑锁，这些锁需要不同的多阶段推理和操作策略。这些LLM生成的规则会产生非马尔可夫和长时程任务，需要时间建模和基于记忆的推理。我们进一步提出了VQ-Memory，一种紧凑且结构化的时间表示，它使用向量量化变分自编码器 (VQ-VAEs) 将过去的本体感受状态编码为离散的潜在令牌。这种表示方法在保留高层任务阶段上下文的同时，过滤了低层噪声，从而提供了轻量级但稳健的时间线索，与现有的视觉-语言-动作模型 (VLA) 兼容。对最先进的VLA模型和扩散策略进行的大量实验表明，VQ-Memory持续改进长时程规划，增强了对未见配置的泛化能力，并以降低的计算成本实现了更高效的操作。项目网页：vqmemory.github.io

TLDR: The paper introduces RuleSafe, a new non-Markovian robotics benchmark involving safe unlocking, and proposes VQ-Memory, a VQ-VAE based temporal representation, to improve long-horizon planning in manipulation tasks within this benchmark.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一个新的非马尔可夫机器人基准测试 RuleSafe，涉及保险箱解锁；并提出 VQ-Memory， 一种基于 VQ-VAE 的时间表示方法，以提高该基准测试中操纵任务的长期规划能力。

Authors: Wang Honghui, Jing Zhi, Ao Jicong, Song Shiji, Li Xuelong, Huang Gao, Bai Chenjia

See, Plan, Rewind: Progress-Aware Vision-Language-Action Models for Robust Robotic Manipulation

Abstract (English): Measurement of task progress through explicit, actionable milestones is critical for robust robotic manipulation. This progress awareness enables a model to ground its current task status, anticipate verifiable intermediate states, and detect and recover from failures when progress stalls. To embody this capability, we introduce See, Plan, Rewind (SPR), a progress-aware vision-language-action framework that dynamically grounds language instructions into a sequence of spatial subgoals. SPR operates through a continuous core cycle, Seeing the current state and upcoming milestone, Planning a trajectory towards the next 2D waypoint, and Rewinding to a recoverable state upon failure by monitoring progress against the expected sequence. This closed-loop approach enables robust error correction without requiring additional training data or auxiliary models. Extensive experiments demonstrate the framework's effectiveness, generalization and robustness: SPR outperforms the MolmoAct baseline by 5\% on the LIBERO benchmark. On the challenging LIBERO-Plus benchmark with unseen instructions and initial states, SPR achieves state-of-the-art robustness with the smallest performance drop, surpassing OpenVLA-OFT and UniVLA, demonstrating superior out-of-distribution robustness.

摘要（中文）: 通过明确、可执行的里程碑来衡量任务进度对于鲁棒的机器人操作至关重要。这种进度感知能力使模型能够确定其当前任务状态，预测可验证的中间状态，并在进度停滞时检测并从故障中恢复。为了体现这种能力，我们引入了一种进度感知的视觉-语言-动作框架，称为“看、计划、回溯”（SPR），该框架能够将语言指令动态地转化为一系列空间子目标。SPR通过一个连续的核心循环运作，即“看”：观察当前状态和即将到来的里程碑；“计划”：规划一条通往下一个二维航点的轨迹；以及“回溯”：通过监控与预期序列的进度来在失败时回溯到可恢复的状态。这种闭环方法无需额外的训练数据或辅助模型即可实现稳健的错误纠正。大量的实验证明了该框架的有效性、泛化性和鲁棒性：SPR在LIBERO基准上比MolmoAct基线高出5%。在具有未见指令和初始状态的具有挑战性的LIBERO-Plus基准上，SPR实现了最先进的鲁棒性，性能下降幅度最小，超过了OpenVLA-OFT和UniVLA，展现了卓越的分布外鲁棒性。

TLDR: The paper introduces See, Plan, Rewind (SPR), a progress-aware vision-language-action framework for robust robotic manipulation that uses a closed-loop approach for error correction based on tracking progress towards spatial subgoals. It demonstrated better performance and robustness than existing VLA methods in robotic manipulation benchmarks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了See, Plan, Rewind (SPR)，一个具有进展意识的视觉-语言-动作框架，用于鲁棒的机器人操作，它使用闭环方法，通过跟踪达到空间子目标的进展来进行错误校正。它在机器人操作基准测试中表现出比现有VLA方法更好的性能和鲁棒性。

Authors: Tingjun Dai, Mingfei Han, Tingwen Du, Zhiheng Liu, Zhihui Li, Salman Khan, Jun Yu, Xiaojun Chang

Implicit Geometry Representations for Vision-and-Language Navigation from Web Videos

Abstract (English): Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) has long been constrained by the limited diversity and scalability of simulator-curated datasets, which fail to capture the complexity of real-world environments. To overcome this limitation, we introduce a large-scale video-instruction framework derived from web-based room tour videos, enabling agents to learn from natural human walking demonstrations in diverse, realistic indoor settings. Unlike existing datasets, our framework integrates both open-ended description-enriched trajectories and action-enriched trajectories reconstructed in 3D, providing richer spatial and semantic supervision. A key extension in this work is the incorporation of implicit geometry representations, which extract spatial cues directly from RGB frames without requiring fragile 3D reconstruction. This approach substantially improves data utilization, alleviates reconstruction failures, and unlocks large portions of previously unusable video data. Comprehensive experiments across multiple VLN benchmarks (CVDN, SOON, R2R, and REVERIE) demonstrate that our method not only sets new state-of-the-art performance but also enables the development of robust zero-shot navigation agents. By bridging large-scale web videos with implicit spatial reasoning, this work advances embodied navigation towards more scalable, generalizable, and real-world applicable solutions.

摘要（中文）: 视觉语言导航（VLN）长期以来受到模拟器生成数据集在多样性和可扩展性方面的限制，这些数据集无法捕捉真实世界环境的复杂性。为了克服这一限制，我们引入了一个大规模视频-指令框架，该框架源自于网络上的房间漫游视频，使智能体能够从多样化、真实的室内环境中自然的人类行走演示中学习。与现有数据集不同，我们的框架集成了源于开放式的描述性增强轨迹以及在3D中重建的行动增强轨迹，提供了更丰富的空间和语义监督。本研究的一个关键扩展是结合了隐式几何表示，它直接从RGB帧中提取空间线索，而无需脆弱的3D重建。这种方法大大提高了数据利用率，减轻了重建失败问题，并解锁了大量以前无法使用的视频数据。在多个VLN基准测试（CVDN、SOON、R2R和REVERIE）上的全面实验表明，我们的方法不仅创造了新的最先进性能，而且还能够开发出鲁棒的零样本导航智能体。通过将大规模网络视频与隐式空间推理相结合，这项工作推动了具身导航向更具可扩展性、通用性和适用于现实世界的解决方案发展。

TLDR: The paper introduces a new Vision-Language Navigation (VLN) framework using web-based room tour videos and implicit geometry representations to overcome limitations of existing simulator-based datasets, achieving state-of-the-art performance and enabling robust zero-shot navigation.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种新的视觉语言导航（VLN）框架，该框架使用基于网络的房间游览视频和隐式几何表示，克服了现有基于模拟器的数据集的局限性，实现了最先进的性能，并实现了强大的零样本导航。

Authors: Mingfei Han, Haihong Hao, Liang Ma, Kamila Zhumakhanova, Ekaterina Radionova, Jingyi Zhang, Xiaojun Chang, Xiaodan Liang, Ivan Laptev

SPAN-Nav: Generalized Spatial Awareness for Versatile Vision-Language Navigation

Abstract (English): Recent embodied navigation approaches leveraging Vision-Language Models (VLMs) demonstrate strong generalization in versatile Vision-Language Navigation (VLN). However, reliable path planning in complex environments remains challenging due to insufficient spatial awareness. In this work, we introduce SPAN-Nav, an end-to-end foundation model designed to infuse embodied navigation with universal 3D spatial awareness using RGB video streams. SPAN-Nav extracts spatial priors across diverse scenes through an occupancy prediction task on extensive indoor and outdoor environments. To mitigate the computational burden, we introduce a compact representation for spatial priors, finding that a single token is sufficient to encapsulate the coarse-grained cues essential for navigation tasks. Furthermore, inspired by the Chain-of-Thought (CoT) mechanism, SPAN-Nav utilizes this single spatial token to explicitly inject spatial cues into action reasoning through an end-to end framework. Leveraging multi-task co-training, SPAN-Nav captures task-adaptive cues from generalized spatial priors, enabling robust spatial awareness to generalize even to the task lacking explicit spatial supervision. To support comprehensive spatial learning, we present a massive dataset of 4.2 million occupancy annotations that covers both indoor and outdoor scenes across multi-type navigation tasks. SPAN-Nav achieves state-of-the-art performance across three benchmarks spanning diverse scenarios and varied navigation tasks. Finally, real-world experiments validate the robust generalization and practical reliability of our approach across complex physical scenarios.

摘要（中文）: 最近利用视觉-语言模型(VLMs)的具身导航方法在多用途视觉-语言导航（VLN）中表现出强大的泛化能力。然而，由于缺乏足够的空间感知能力，在复杂环境中进行可靠的路径规划仍然具有挑战性。在这项工作中，我们引入了SPAN-Nav，一个端到端的基础模型，旨在使用RGB视频流将通用的3D空间感知注入到具身导航中。SPAN-Nav通过对广泛的室内和室外环境中的占据预测任务，提取跨不同场景的空间先验。为了减轻计算负担，我们引入了一种紧凑的空间先验表示方法，发现单个token足以封装导航任务所需的高层次线索。此外，受链式思考(CoT)机制的启发，SPAN-Nav利用这个单个空间token，通过一个端到端框架，将空间线索显式地注入到动作推理中。通过多任务协同训练，SPAN-Nav从广义空间先验中捕获任务自适应线索，从而实现鲁棒的空间感知，即使在缺乏显式空间监督的任务中也能泛化。为了支持全面的空间学习，我们提出了一个包含420万个占据标注的大规模数据集，该数据集涵盖了跨多种导航任务的室内和室外场景。SPAN-Nav在涵盖不同场景和不同导航任务的三个基准测试上实现了最先进的性能。最后，真实世界的实验验证了我们的方法在复杂物理场景中的鲁棒泛化能力和实际可靠性。

TLDR: SPAN-Nav introduces a foundation model that enhances vision-language navigation with universal 3D spatial awareness by predicting occupancy from RGB video and injecting spatial cues into action reasoning, achieving state-of-the-art performance across diverse benchmarks.

简要说明: SPAN-Nav 介绍了一种基础模型，通过预测 RGB 视频的占据情况并将空间线索注入到动作推理中，增强了视觉语言导航的通用 3D 空间感知能力，并在各种基准测试中实现了最先进的性能。

Authors: Jiahang Liu, Tianyu Xu, Jiawei Chen, Lu Yue, Jiazhao Zhang, Zhiyong Wang, Minghan Li, Qisheng Zhao, Anqi Li, Qi Su, Zhizheng Zhang, He Wang

Latent World Models for Automated Driving: A Unified Taxonomy, Evaluation Framework, and Open Challenges

Abstract (English): Emerging generative world models and vision-language-action (VLA) systems are rapidly reshaping automated driving by enabling scalable simulation, long-horizon forecasting, and capability-rich decision making. Across these directions, latent representations serve as the central computational substrate: they compress high-dimensional multi-sensor observations, enable temporally coherent rollouts, and provide interfaces for planning, reasoning, and controllable generation. This paper proposes a unifying latent-space framework that synthesizes recent progress in world models for automated driving. The framework organizes the design space by the target and form of latent representations (latent worlds, latent actions, latent generators; continuous states, discrete tokens, and hybrids) and by structural priors for geometry, topology, and semantics. Building on this taxonomy, the paper articulates five cross-cutting internal mechanics (i.e, structural isomorphism, long-horizon temporal stability, semantic and reasoning alignment, value-aligned objectives and post-training, as well as adaptive computation and deliberation) and connects these design choices to robustness, generalization, and deployability. The work also proposes concrete evaluation prescriptions, including a closed-loop metric suite and a resource-aware deliberation cost, designed to reduce the open-loop / closed-loop mismatch. Finally, the paper identifies actionable research directions toward advancing latent world model for decision-ready, verifiable, and resource-efficient automated driving.

摘要（中文）: 涌现的生成式世界模型和视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 系统正在通过实现可扩展的仿真、长时程预测以及功能丰富的决策，迅速重塑自动驾驶。在这些方向中，潜在表征作为核心计算基底：它们压缩高维多传感器观测，实现时间上连贯的展开，并为规划、推理和可控生成提供接口。本文提出了一个统一的潜在空间框架，该框架综合了自动驾驶世界模型的最新进展。该框架通过潜在表示的目标和形式（潜在世界、潜在动作、潜在生成器；连续状态、离散令牌和混合形式）以及几何、拓扑和语义的结构先验性来组织设计空间。基于这个分类法，本文阐述了五个贯穿始终的内部机制（即结构同构、长时程时间稳定性、语义和推理对齐、价值对齐目标和后训练，以及自适应计算和审议），并将这些设计选择与鲁棒性、泛化性和可部署性联系起来。该研究还提出了具体的评估方案，包括一个闭环指标套件和一个资源感知的审议成本，旨在减少开环/闭环不匹配。最后，本文确定了可操作的研究方向，以推进用于决策就绪、可验证和资源高效的自动驾驶的潜在世界模型。

TLDR: This paper presents a unifying framework and taxonomy for latent world models in automated driving, focusing on representations, internal mechanics, and evaluation, while also identifying open research challenges.

简要说明: 本文提出了一个统一的框架和分类法，用于自动驾驶中潜在的世界模型，重点关注表征、内部机制和评价，同时也识别了开放的研究挑战。

Relevance: (10/10)
Novelty: (7/10)

Authors: Rongxiang Zeng, Yongqi Dong

PlayWorld: Learning Robot World Models from Autonomous Play

Abstract (English): Action-conditioned video models offer a promising path to building general-purpose robot simulators that can improve directly from data. Yet, despite training on large-scale robot datasets, current state-of-the-art video models still struggle to predict physically consistent robot-object interactions that are crucial in robotic manipulation. To close this gap, we present PlayWorld, a simple, scalable, and fully autonomous pipeline for training high-fidelity video world simulators from interaction experience. In contrast to prior approaches that rely on success-biased human demonstrations, PlayWorld is the first system capable of learning entirely from unsupervised robot self-play, enabling naturally scalable data collection while capturing complex, long-tailed physical interactions essential for modeling realistic object dynamics. Experiments across diverse manipulation tasks show that PlayWorld generates high-quality, physically consistent predictions for contact-rich interactions that are not captured by world models trained on human-collected data. We further demonstrate the versatility of PlayWorld in enabling fine-grained failure prediction and policy evaluation, with up to 40% improvements over human-collected data. Finally, we demonstrate how PlayWorld enables reinforcement learning in the world model, improving policy performance by 65% in success rates when deployed in the real world.

摘要（中文）: 以动作为条件的视频模型为构建通用机器人仿真器提供了一条有希望的途径，这些仿真器可以直接从数据中改进。然而，尽管在大型机器人数据集上进行训练，当前最先进的视频模型仍然难以预测物理上一致的机器人-物体交互，而这对于机器人操作至关重要。为了弥合这一差距，我们提出了 PlayWorld，这是一个简单、可扩展且完全自主的流程，用于从交互经验中训练高保真视频世界仿真器。与依赖于成功偏见的人工示教的先前方法不同，PlayWorld 是第一个能够完全从无监督的机器人自博弈中学习的系统，从而实现自然可扩展的数据收集，同时捕获建模真实物体动力学所必需的复杂、长尾物理交互。跨越多种操作任务的实验表明，PlayWorld 为富含接触的交互产生高质量、物理上一致的预测，而这些交互无法被在人工采集数据上训练的世界模型捕获。我们进一步证明了 PlayWorld 在实现精细化故障预测和策略评估方面的多功能性，相比人工采集的数据，性能提升高达 40%。最后，我们展示了 PlayWorld 如何在世界模型中实现强化学习，从而在现实世界部署时将策略性能的成功率提高 65%。

TLDR: The paper introduces PlayWorld, a system for training robot world models entirely from unsupervised robot self-play, demonstrating improved physical consistency and performance in manipulation tasks compared to models trained on human-collected data. It enables fine-grained failure prediction, policy evaluation, and improves RL policy performance in the real world.

简要说明: 本文介绍了一种名为 PlayWorld 的系统，该系统完全基于无监督的机器人自主操作来训练机器人世界模型。相比于基于人类数据训练的模型，该系统在操纵任务中表现出更好的物理一致性和性能。它能够进行细粒度的故障预测和策略评估，并提高了强化学习策略在真实世界中的性能。

Authors: Tenny Yin, Zhiting Mei, Zhonghe Zheng, Miyu Yamane, David Wang, Jade Sceats, Samuel M. Bateman, Lihan Zha, Apurva Badithela, Ola Shorinwa, Anirudha Majumdar

OOD-MMSafe: Advancing MLLM Safety from Harmful Intent to Hidden Consequences

Abstract (English): While safety alignment for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) has gained significant attention, current paradigms primarily target malicious intent or situational violations. We propose shifting the safety frontier toward consequence-driven safety, a paradigm essential for the robust deployment of autonomous and embodied agents. To formalize this shift, we introduce OOD-MMSafe, a benchmark comprising 455 curated query-image pairs designed to evaluate a model's ability to identify latent hazards within context-dependent causal chains. Our analysis reveals a pervasive causal blindness among frontier models, with the highest 67.5% failure rate in high-capacity closed-source models, and identifies a preference ceiling where static alignment yields format-centric failures rather than improved safety reasoning as model capacity grows. To address these bottlenecks, we develop the Consequence-Aware Safety Policy Optimization (CASPO) framework, which integrates the model's intrinsic reasoning as a dynamic reference for token-level self-distillation rewards. Experimental results demonstrate that CASPO significantly enhances consequence projection, reducing the failure ratio of risk identification to 7.3% for Qwen2.5-VL-7B and 5.7% for Qwen3-VL-4B while maintaining overall effectiveness.

摘要（中文）: 多模态大型语言模型(MLLM)的安全对齐已获得显著关注，但目前的范式主要针对恶意意图或情景违规。我们提出将安全前沿转移到后果驱动的安全，这是一种对于自主及具身智能体的稳健部署至关重要的范式。为了形式化这种转变，我们引入了OOD-MMSafe，一个包含455个精心设计的查询-图像对的基准，旨在评估模型在上下文相关的因果链中识别潜在风险的能力。我们的分析揭示了前沿模型中普遍存在的因果盲区，其中高容量闭源模型的失败率最高，达到67.5%。我们还发现了一个偏好上限，在此上限处，静态对齐会产生以格式为中心的失败，而非随着模型容量的增长而改善安全推理。为了解决这些瓶颈，我们开发了后果感知安全策略优化(CASPO)框架，该框架将模型的内在推理作为令牌级自蒸馏奖励的动态参考。实验结果表明，CASPO显著增强了后果预测，将Qwen2.5-VL-7B的风险识别失败率降低至7.3%，将Qwen3-VL-4B的风险识别失败率降低至5.7%，同时保持了整体有效性。

TLDR: The paper introduces OOD-MMSafe, a benchmark to evaluate MLLMs' ability to identify latent hazards in causal chains, and proposes CASPO, a framework to improve consequence projection, leading to significant reduction in failure rates on risk identification.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了OOD-MMSafe，一个旨在评估MLLM识别因果链中潜在危害能力的基准，并提出了CASPO框架，以提高结果预测，从而显著降低风险识别的失败率。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)

Authors: Ming Wen, Kun Yang, Jingyu Zhang, Yuxuan Liu, shiwen cui, Shouling Ji, Xingjun Ma

EvoDriveVLA: Evolving Autonomous Driving Vision-Language-Action Model via Collaborative Perception-Planning Distillation

Abstract (English): Vision-Language-Action models have shown great promise for autonomous driving, yet they suffer from degraded perception after unfreezing the visual encoder and struggle with accumulated instability in long-term planning. To address these challenges, we propose EvoDriveVLA-a novel collaborative perception-planning distillation framework that integrates self-anchored perceptual constraints and oracle-guided trajectory optimization. Specifically, self-anchored visual distillation leverages self-anchor teacher to deliver visual anchoring constraints, regularizing student representations via trajectory-guided key-region awareness. In parallel, oracle-guided trajectory distillation employs a future-aware oracle teacher with coarse-to-fine trajectory refinement and Monte Carlo dropout sampling to produce high-quality trajectory candidates, thereby selecting the optimal trajectory to guide the student's prediction. EvoDriveVLA achieves SOTA performance in open-loop evaluation and significantly enhances performance in closed-loop evaluation. Our code is available at: https://github.com/hey-cjj/EvoDriveVLA.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言-动作模型在自动驾驶领域展现了巨大潜力，然而，解冻视觉编码器后，它们会遭受感知能力退化的问题，并在长期规划中面临累积不稳定性。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了EvoDriveVLA——一种新型的协同感知-规划蒸馏框架，该框架集成了自锚感知约束和专家引导轨迹优化。具体而言，自锚视觉蒸馏利用自锚定教师传递视觉锚定约束，通过轨迹引导的关键区域感知来规范化学生的表征。同时，专家引导轨迹蒸馏采用具备未来感知能力的专家教师，通过由粗到精的轨迹细化和蒙特卡洛Dropout采样来生成高质量的轨迹候选项，从而选择最优轨迹来指导学生的预测。EvoDriveVLA在开环评估中实现了SOTA性能，并在闭环评估中显著提高了性能。我们的代码已在以下网址提供：https://github.com/hey-cjj/EvoDriveVLA.

TLDR: EvoDriveVLA addresses perception degradation and planning instability in autonomous driving vision-language-action models through a collaborative distillation framework using self-anchored perceptual constraints and oracle-guided trajectory optimization, achieving SOTA performance.

简要说明: EvoDriveVLA通过协作蒸馏框架解决了自动驾驶视觉-语言-动作模型中感知退化和规划不稳定的问题，该框架利用自锚定感知约束和oracle引导的轨迹优化，实现了SOTA性能。

Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Jiajun Cao, Xiaoan Zhang, Xiaobao Wei, Liyuqiu Huang, Wang Zijian, Hanzhen Zhang, Zhengyu Jia, Wei Mao, Hao Wang, Xianming Liu, Shuchang Zhou Liu, Yang Wang, Shanghang Zhang

LAP: A Language-Aware Planning Model For Procedure Planning In Instructional Videos

Abstract (English): Procedure planning requires a model to predict a sequence of actions that transform a start visual observation into a goal in instructional videos. While most existing methods rely primarily on visual observations as input, they often struggle with the inherent ambiguity where different actions can appear visually similar. In this work, we argue that language descriptions offer a more distinctive representation in the latent space for procedure planning. We introduce Language-Aware Planning (LAP), a novel method that leverages the expressiveness of language to bridge visual observation and planning. LAP uses a finetuned Vision Language Model (VLM) to translate visual observations into text descriptions and to predict actions and extract text embeddings. These text embeddings are more distinctive than visual embeddings and are used in a diffusion model for planning action sequences. We evaluate LAP on three procedure planning benchmarks: CrossTask, Coin, and NIV. LAP achieves new state-of-the-art performance across multiple metrics and time horizons by large margin, demonstrating the significant advantage of language-aware planning.

摘要（中文）: 过程规划需要一个模型来预测一系列动作，这些动作将起始视觉观察转化为教学视频中的目标。虽然大多数现有方法主要依赖于视觉观察作为输入，但它们常常难以应对固有的歧义性，即不同的动作可能在视觉上看起来相似。在这项工作中，我们认为语言描述在程序规划的潜在空间中提供更具区别性的表示。我们引入了语言感知规划 (LAP)，这是一种新颖的方法，它利用语言的表达能力来桥接视觉观察和规划。 LAP 使用微调的视觉语言模型 (VLM) 将视觉观察转化为文本描述，并预测动作和提取文本嵌入。这些文本嵌入比视觉嵌入更具区别性，并被用于扩散模型中进行动作序列规划。我们在三个过程规划基准测试上评估 LAP：CrossTask、Coin 和 NIV。 LAP 在多个指标和时间跨度上大幅度实现了新的最先进性能，展示了语言感知规划的显著优势。

TLDR: The paper introduces Language-Aware Planning (LAP), a method using a finetuned VLM to translate visual observations into text descriptions for improved procedure planning in instructional videos, achieving SOTA results.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了语言感知规划(LAP)，它使用微调的VLM将视觉观察转化为文本描述，以改进教学视频中的程序规划，并取得了最先进的结果。

Authors: Lei Shi, Victor Aregbede, Andreas Persson, Martin Längkvist, Amy Loutfi, Stephanie Lowry

Point Cloud as a Foreign Language for Multi-modal Large Language Model

Abstract (English): Multi-modal large language models (MLLMs) have shown remarkable progress in integrating visual and linguistic understanding. Recent efforts have extended these capabilities to 3D understanding through encoder-based architectures that rely on pre-trained 3D encoders to extract geometric features. However, such approaches suffer from semantic misalignment between geometric and linguistic spaces, resolution sensitivity, and substantial computational overhead. In this work, we present SAGE, the first end-to-end 3D MLLM that directly processes raw point clouds without relying on a pre-trained 3D encoder. Our approach introduces a lightweight 3D tokenizer that combines geometric sampling and neighbourhood aggregation with vector quantization to convert point clouds into discrete tokens--treating 3D data as a foreign language that naturally extends the LLM's vocabulary. Furthermore, to enhance the model's reasoning capability on complex 3D tasks, we propose a preference optimization training strategy with a semantic alignment-based reward, specifically designed for open-ended 3D question answering where responses are descriptive. Extensive experiments across diverse 3D understanding benchmarks demonstrate that our end-to-end approach outperforms existing encoder-based methods while offering significant advantages in computational efficiency, generalization across LLM backbones, and robustness to input resolution variations. Code is available at: github.com/snehaputul/SAGE3D.

摘要（中文）: 多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）在整合视觉和语言理解方面展现了显著的进展。近期的工作通过基于编码器的架构，依赖于预训练的3D编码器提取几何特征，将这些能力扩展到3D理解。然而，这些方法受到几何和语言空间之间的语义不对齐、分辨率敏感性以及巨大的计算开销的困扰。在这项工作中，我们提出了SAGE，首个端到端的3D MLLM，它直接处理原始点云，无需依赖预训练的3D编码器。我们的方法引入了一个轻量级的3D分词器，将几何采样和邻域聚合与矢量量化相结合，将点云转换为离散tokens——将3D数据视为一种外语，自然地扩展了LLM的词汇表。此外，为了增强模型处理复杂3D任务的推理能力，我们提出了一种基于语义对齐奖励的偏好优化训练策略，专门为描述性开放式3D问答而设计。在各种3D理解基准上的广泛实验表明，我们的端到端方法优于现有的基于编码器的方法，同时在计算效率、跨LLM主干的泛化能力以及对输入分辨率变化的鲁棒性方面提供了显著优势。代码可在 github.com/snehaputul/SAGE3D 获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces SAGE, the first end-to-end 3D MLLM that directly processes raw point clouds by tokenizing them into a foreign language for LLMs, achieving superior performance and efficiency compared to encoder-based methods.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了SAGE，它是第一个端到端的3D MLLM，通过将原始点云标记化为LLM的外语来直接处理原始点云，从而实现了比基于编码器的方法更好的性能和效率。

Authors: Sneha Paul, Zachary Patterson, Nizar Bouguila

TiPToP: A Modular Open-Vocabulary Planning System for Robotic Manipulation

Abstract (English): We present TiPToP, an extensible modular system that combines pretrained vision foundation models with an existing Task and Motion Planner (TAMP) to solve multi-step manipulation tasks directly from input RGB images and natural-language instructions. Our system aims to be simple and easy-to-use: it can be installed and run on a standard DROID setup in under one hour and adapted to new embodiments with minimal effort. We evaluate TiPToP -- which requires zero robot data -- over 28 tabletop manipulation tasks in simulation and the real world and find it matches or outperforms $π_{0.5}\text{-DROID}$, a vision-language-action (VLA) model fine-tuned on 350 hours of embodiment-specific demonstrations. TiPToP's modular architecture enables us to analyze the system's failure modes at the component level. We analyze results from an evaluation of 173 trials and identify directions for improvement. We release TiPToP open-source to further research on modular manipulation systems and tighter integration between learning and planning. Project website and code: https://tiptop-robot.github.io

摘要（中文）: 我们提出了 TiPToP，一个可扩展的模块化系统，它将预训练的视觉基础模型与现有的任务和运动规划器 (TAMP) 相结合，直接从输入的 RGB 图像和自然语言指令中解决多步操作任务。我们的系统旨在简单易用：它可以在不到一小时内安装并在标准 DROID 设置上运行，并且可以以最小的努力适应新的机器人形态。我们评估了 TiPToP——它不需要任何机器人数据——在模拟和真实世界的 28 个桌面操作任务中，发现它与 $π_{0.5}\text{-DROID}$ 相当或优于 $π_{0.5}\text{-DROID}$，后者是一个在 350 小时的特定于机器人的演示数据上微调的视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型。TiPToP 的模块化架构使我们能够在组件级别分析系统的失效模式。我们分析了 173 个试验的评估结果，并确定了改进的方向。我们开源发布 TiPToP，以进一步研究模块化操作系统以及学习和规划之间更紧密的集成。项目网站和代码：https://tiptop-robot.github.io

TLDR: TiPToP is a modular, open-vocabulary robotic manipulation system leveraging pretrained vision foundation models and Task and Motion Planning (TAMP) to solve manipulation tasks from RGB images and natural language, outperforming a fine-tuned VLA model without robot-specific training data.

简要说明: TiPToP是一个模块化的、开放词汇的机器人操作系统，利用预训练的视觉基础模型和任务与运动规划（TAMP），通过RGB图像和自然语言解决操作任务，性能优于经过微调的VLA模型，且无需机器人特定的训练数据。

Authors: William Shen, Nishanth Kumar, Sahit Chintalapudi, Jie Wang, Christopher Watson, Edward Hu, Jing Cao, Dinesh Jayaraman, Leslie Pack Kaelbling, Tomás Lozano-Pérez

Emerging Extrinsic Dexterity in Cluttered Scenes via Dynamics-aware Policy Learning

Abstract (English): Extrinsic dexterity leverages environmental contact to overcome the limitations of prehensile manipulation. However, achieving such dexterity in cluttered scenes remains challenging and underexplored, as it requires selectively exploiting contact among multiple interacting objects with inherently coupled dynamics. Existing approaches lack explicit modeling of such complex dynamics and therefore fall short in non-prehensile manipulation in cluttered environments, which in turn limits their practical applicability in real-world environments. In this paper, we introduce a Dynamics-Aware Policy Learning (DAPL) framework that can facilitate policy learning with a learned representation of contact-induced object dynamics in cluttered environments. This representation is learned through explicit world modeling and used to condition reinforcement learning, enabling extrinsic dexterity to emerge without hand-crafted contact heuristics or complex reward shaping. We evaluate our approach in both simulation and the real world. Our method outperforms prehensile manipulation, human teleoperation, and prior representation-based policies by over 25% in success rate on unseen simulated cluttered scenes with varying densities. The real-world success rate reaches around 50% across 10 cluttered scenes, while a practical grocery deployment further demonstrates robust sim-to-real transfer and applicability.

摘要（中文）: 外在灵巧性利用环境接触来克服抓取操作的局限性。然而，在杂乱场景中实现这种灵巧性仍然具有挑战性且尚未被充分探索，因为它需要有选择性地利用多个相互作用对象之间的接触，这些对象具有内在耦合的动力学。现有方法缺乏对此类复杂动力学的显式建模，因此在杂乱环境中的非抓取操作方面表现不足，这反过来限制了它们在现实世界环境中的实际适用性。在本文中，我们介绍了一种动力学感知策略学习（DAPL）框架，该框架可以通过学习到的杂乱环境中接触引起的物体动力学表示来促进策略学习。这种表示是通过显式的世界建模学习得到的，并用于调节强化学习，从而使得外在灵巧性得以涌现，而无需手工制作的接触启发式方法或复杂的奖励塑造。我们在仿真和真实世界中评估了我们的方法。在具有不同密度的、未见过的模拟杂乱场景中，我们的方法在成功率方面优于抓取操作、人工遥操作和先前的基于表示的策略至少25%。在10个杂乱场景中，真实世界的成功率达到约50%，而实际的杂货部署进一步证明了稳健的仿真到真实世界的迁移和适用性。

TLDR: This paper introduces a Dynamics-Aware Policy Learning (DAPL) framework for robot manipulation that learns contact-induced object dynamics in cluttered environments. It uses this learned representation to improve reinforcement learning policies, demonstrating superior performance in simulation and real-world cluttered scenes compared to other methods.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种动态感知策略学习（DAPL）框架，用于机器人操作，该框架可以学习杂乱环境中接触诱导的物体动力学。它利用这种学习到的表示来改进强化学习策略，在模拟和真实世界的杂乱场景中，其性能优于其他方法。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yixin Zheng, Jiangran Lyu, Yifan Zhang, Jiayi Chen, Mi Yan, Yuntian Deng, Xuesong Shi, Xiaoguang Zhao, Yizhou Wang, Zhizheng Zhang, He Wang

Let's Reward Step-by-Step: Step-Aware Contrastive Alignment for Vision-Language Navigation in Continuous Environments

Abstract (English): Vision-Language Navigation in Continuous Environments (VLN-CE) requires agents to learn complex reasoning from long-horizon human interactions. While Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have driven recent progress, current training paradigms struggle to balance generalization capability, error recovery and training stability. Specifically, (i) policies derived from SFT suffer from compounding errors, struggling to recover from out-of-distribution states, and (ii) Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RFT) methods e.g. GRPO are bottlenecked by sparse outcome rewards. Their binary feedback fails to assign credit to individual steps, leading to gradient signal collapse in failure dominant batches. To address these challenges, we introduce Step-Aware Contrastive Alignment (SACA), a framework designed to extract dense supervision from imperfect trajectories. At its core, the Perception-Grounded Step-Aware auditor evaluates progress step-by-step, disentangling failed trajectories into valid prefixes and exact divergence points. Leveraging these signals, Scenario-Conditioned Group Construction mechanism dynamically routes batches to specialized resampling and optimization strategies. Extensive experiments on VLN-CE benchmarks demonstrate that SACA achieves state-of-the-art performance.

摘要（中文）: 在连续环境下的视觉-语言导航（VLN-CE）要求智能体从长程人类交互中学习复杂的推理。虽然多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）推动了最近的进展，但当前的训练范式难以平衡泛化能力、错误恢复能力和训练稳定性。具体来说，（i）源自监督微调（SFT）的策略会遭受误差累积的问题，难以从分布外的状态中恢复；（ii）强化微调（RFT）方法，例如GRPO，受到稀疏结果奖励的限制。它们的二元反馈无法将功劳分配给单个步骤，导致在失败占主导地位的批次中出现梯度信号崩溃。为了应对这些挑战，我们引入了步骤感知的对比对齐（SACA），这是一个旨在从不完美的轨迹中提取密集监督的框架。其核心在于，感知接地的步骤感知审核器逐步骤地评估进展，将失败的轨迹解耦为有效的轨迹前缀和精确的偏差点。利用这些信号，情境条件下的组构建机制会将批次动态地路由到专门的重采样和优化策略。在VLN-CE基准上的大量实验表明，SACA实现了最先进的性能。

TLDR: This paper introduces a Step-Aware Contrastive Alignment (SACA) framework for Vision-Language Navigation in Continuous Environments (VLN-CE) that uses step-by-step progress evaluation and specialized resampling/optimization strategies to address challenges related to error recovery and sparse rewards, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种用于连续环境中的视觉-语言导航（VLN-CE）的步进式对比对齐（SACA）框架。该框架通过逐步评估进度以及专门的重采样和优化策略，来解决与错误恢复和稀疏奖励相关的挑战，并取得了最先进的性能。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Haoyuan Li, Rui Liu, Hehe Fan, Yi Yang

Robotic Scene Cloning:Advancing Zero-Shot Robotic Scene Adaptation in Manipulation via Visual Prompt Editing

Abstract (English): Modern robots can perform a wide range of simple tasks and adapt to diverse scenarios in the well-trained environment. However, deploying pre-trained robot models in real-world user scenarios remains challenging due to their limited zero-shot capabilities, often necessitating extensive on-site data collection. To address this issue, we propose Robotic Scene Cloning (RSC), a novel method designed for scene-specific adaptation by editing existing robot operation trajectories. RSC achieves accurate and scene-consistent sample generation by leveraging a visual prompting mechanism and a carefully tuned condition injection module. Not only transferring textures but also performing moderate shape adaptations in response to the visual prompts, RSC demonstrates reliable task performance across a variety of object types. Experiments across various simulated and real-world environments demonstrate that RSC significantly enhances policy generalization in target environments.

摘要（中文）: 现代机器人可以在训练良好的环境中执行各种简单任务并适应不同的场景。 然而，由于零样本能力的限制，将预训练的机器人模型部署到现实世界的用户场景中仍然具有挑战性，这通常需要大量的现场数据收集。 为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种名为机器人场景克隆 (RSC) 的新方法，旨在通过编辑现有的机器人操作轨迹来实现特定场景的适应。 RSC 通过利用视觉提示机制和精心调整的条件注入模块来实现准确且场景一致的样本生成。 RSC 不仅可以传递纹理，还可以响应视觉提示进行适度的形状调整，从而在各种对象类型上表现出可靠的任务性能。 在各种模拟和真实世界环境中的实验表明，RSC 显著提高了目标环境中策略的泛化能力。

TLDR: The paper introduces Robotic Scene Cloning (RSC), a method to adapt pre-trained robot models to new environments by editing existing trajectories using visual prompting, achieving better zero-shot performance and policy generalization.

简要说明: 该论文提出了 Robotic Scene Cloning (RSC) 方法，通过使用视觉提示编辑现有轨迹来使预训练的机器人模型适应新环境，从而实现了更好的零样本性能和策略泛化。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Binyuan Huang, Yuqing Wen, Yucheng Zhao, Yaosi Hu, Tiancai Wang, Chang Wen Chen, Haoqiang Fan, Zhenzhong Chen

SCDP: Learning Humanoid Locomotion from Partial Observations via Mixed-Observation Distillation

Abstract (English): Distilling humanoid locomotion control from offline datasets into deployable policies remains a challenge, as existing methods rely on privileged full-body states that require complex and often unreliable state estimation. We present Sensor-Conditioned Diffusion Policies (SCDP) that enables humanoid locomotion using only onboard sensors, eliminating the need for explicit state estimation. SCDP decouples sensing from supervision through mixed-observation training: diffusion model conditions on sensor histories while being supervised to predict privileged future state-action trajectories, enforcing the model to infer the motion dynamics under partial observability. We further develop restricted denoising, context distribution alignment, and context-aware attention masking to encourage implicit state estimation within the model and to prevent train-deploy mismatch. We validate SCDP on velocity-commanded locomotion and motion reference tracking tasks. In simulation, SCDP achieves near-perfect success on velocity control (99-100%) and 93% tracking success in AMASS test set, performing comparable to privileged baselines while using only onboard sensors. Finally, we deploy the trained policy on a real G1 humanoid at 50 Hz, demonstrating robust real robot locomotion without external sensing or state estimation.

摘要（中文）: 从离线数据集中提炼人形机器人运动控制策略并将其部署，仍然是一项挑战，因为现有方法依赖于需要复杂且通常不可靠的状态估计的特权全身状态。我们提出了传感器条件扩散策略（SCDP），它仅使用板载传感器即可实现人形机器人运动，从而消除了对显式状态估计的需求。SCDP通过混合观测训练将感知与监督解耦：扩散模型以传感器历史为条件，同时被监督以预测特权的未来状态-动作轨迹，从而迫使模型在部分可观测性下推断运动动力学。我们进一步开发了限制性去噪、上下文分布对齐和上下文感知注意力掩码，以鼓励模型内部的隐式状态估计，并防止训练-部署不匹配。我们在速度指令运动和运动参考跟踪任务上验证了SCDP。在仿真中，SCDP在速度控制方面实现了接近完美的成功率（99-100%），并且在AMASS测试集中实现了93%的跟踪成功率，在仅使用板载传感器的情况下，性能与特权基线相当。最后，我们将训练好的策略以50 Hz部署到真实的G1人形机器人上，展示了无需外部传感或状态估计的稳健的真实机器人运动。

TLDR: The paper presents Sensor-Conditioned Diffusion Policies (SCDP) for humanoid locomotion, enabling control using only onboard sensors by decoupling sensing from supervision through mixed-observation training and several other techniques. It demonstrates successful simulation and real-world deployment on a G1 humanoid.

简要说明: 该论文提出了传感器条件扩散策略 (SCDP) 用于人形机器人运动，通过混合观测训练和其他多种技术，仅使用板载传感器实现控制，将感知与监督分离开来。它展示了在 G1 人形机器人上的成功仿真和现实世界部署。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Milo Carroll, Tianhu Peng, Lingfan Bao, Chengxu Zhou, Zhibin Li

NS-VLA: Towards Neuro-Symbolic Vision-Language-Action Models

Abstract (English): Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models are formulated to ground instructions in visual context and generate action sequences for robotic manipulation. Despite recent progress, VLA models still face challenges in learning related and reusable primitives, reducing reliance on large-scale data and complex architectures, and enabling exploration beyond demonstrations. To address these challenges, we propose a novel Neuro-Symbolic Vision-Language-Action (NS-VLA) framework via online reinforcement learning (RL). It introduces a symbolic encoder to embedding vision and language features and extract structured primitives, utilizes a symbolic solver for data-efficient action sequencing, and leverages online RL to optimize generation via expansive exploration. Experiments on robotic manipulation benchmarks demonstrate that NS-VLA outperforms previous methods in both one-shot training and data-perturbed settings, while simultaneously exhibiting superior zero-shot generalizability, high data efficiency and expanded exploration space. Our code is available.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型旨在将指令锚定于视觉语境中，并生成用于机器人操作的动作序列。 尽管最近取得了进展，VLA 模型在学习相关且可复用的基元、减少对大规模数据和复杂架构的依赖、以及实现超越演示的探索方面仍然面临挑战。 为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的神经-符号视觉-语言-动作 (NS-VLA) 框架，该框架通过在线强化学习 (RL) 实现。 它引入了一个符号编码器，用于嵌入视觉和语言特征并提取结构化基元，利用一个符号求解器用于数据高效的动作排序，并利用在线 RL 通过扩展探索来优化生成。 在机器人操作基准测试上的实验表明，NS-VLA 在一次性训练和数据扰动设置中均优于先前的方法，同时表现出卓越的零样本泛化能力、高数据效率和扩展的探索空间。 我们的代码已开源。

TLDR: The paper presents a neuro-symbolic vision-language-action (NS-VLA) framework that uses online reinforcement learning to address challenges in robotic manipulation by learning reusable primitives, increasing data efficiency, and enabling exploration beyond demonstrations.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一个神经符号视觉-语言-动作（NS-VLA）框架，该框架使用在线强化学习来解决机器人操作中的挑战，通过学习可重用的原语、提高数据效率以及实现超出演示的探索。

Authors: Ziyue Zhu, Shangyang Wu, Shuai Zhao, Zhiqiu Zhao, Shengjie Li, Yi Wang, Fang Li, Haoran Luo

Context-Nav: Context-Driven Exploration and Viewpoint-Aware 3D Spatial Reasoning for Instance Navigation

Abstract (English): Text-goal instance navigation (TGIN) asks an agent to resolve a single, free-form description into actions that reach the correct object instance among same-category distractors. We present \textit{Context-Nav} that elevates long, contextual captions from a local matching cue to a global exploration prior and verifies candidates through 3D spatial reasoning. First, we compute dense text-image alignments for a value map that ranks frontiers -- guiding exploration toward regions consistent with the entire description rather than early detections. Second, upon observing a candidate, we perform a viewpoint-aware relation check: the agent samples plausible observer poses, aligns local frames, and accepts a target only if the spatial relations can be satisfied from at least one viewpoint. The pipeline requires no task-specific training or fine-tuning; we attain state-of-the-art performance on InstanceNav and CoIN-Bench. Ablations show that (i) encoding full captions into the value map avoids wasted motion and (ii) explicit, viewpoint-aware 3D verification prevents semantically plausible but incorrect stops. This suggests that geometry-grounded spatial reasoning is a scalable alternative to heavy policy training or human-in-the-loop interaction for fine-grained instance disambiguation in cluttered 3D scenes.

摘要（中文）: 文本-目标实例导航 (TGIN) 任务要求智能体将单个自由形式的描述解析为一系列动作，从而在同类别干扰物中找到正确的目标对象实例。我们提出了 \textit{Context-Nav}，它将长的语境化描述语从局部匹配线索提升为全局探索先验，并通过 3D 空间推理验证候选目标。首先，我们计算密集的文本-图像对齐以生成一个值图，该值图对前沿进行排序，从而引导探索朝向与整个描述一致的区域，而非早期检测。其次，在观察到候选目标时，我们执行视角感知的关系检查：智能体采样合理的观察者姿态，对齐局部坐标系，并且仅当空间关系能够从至少一个视角满足时才接受目标。该流程不需要特定于任务的训练或微调；我们在 InstanceNav 和 CoIN-Bench 上取得了最先进的性能。消融实验表明：(i) 将完整描述语编码到值图中避免了浪费的运动；(ii) 显式的、视角感知的3D验证防止了在语义上合理但并不正确的停止。这表明，几何约束的空间推理是解决复杂 3D 场景中细粒度实例消歧问题的一种可扩展替代方案，可以避免繁重的策略训练或人机交互。

TLDR: The paper introduces Context-Nav, a novel approach for text-goal instance navigation that leverages contextual captions for exploration and viewpoint-aware 3D spatial reasoning for candidate verification without task-specific training, achieving SOTA results.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 Context-Nav，一种新颖的文本目标实例导航方法，利用上下文标题进行探索，并利用视角感知的 3D 空间推理进行候选验证，无需特定于任务的训练，并取得了 SOTA 结果。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Won Shik Jang, Ue-Hwan Kim

StyleVLA: Driving Style-Aware Vision Language Action Model for Autonomous Driving

Abstract (English): Vision Language Models (VLMs) bridge visual perception and linguistic reasoning. In Autonomous Driving (AD), this synergy has enabled Vision Language Action (VLA) models, which translate high-level multimodal understanding into driving behaviors, typically represented as future trajectories. However, existing VLA models mainly generate generic collision-free trajectories. Beyond collision avoidance, adapting to diverse driving styles (e.g., sporty, comfortable) is essential for personalized driving. Moreover, many methods treat trajectory generation as naive token prediction, which can produce kinematically infeasible actions. To address these limitations, we present StyleVLA, a physics-informed VLA framework for generating diverse and physically plausible driving behaviors. We introduce a hybrid loss that combines a kinematic consistency constraint with a continuous regression head to improve trajectory feasibility. To train StyleVLA, built on Qwen3-VL-4B, we construct a large-scale instruction dataset with over 1.2k scenarios, 76k Bird's Eye View (BEV) samples, and 42k First Person View (FPV) samples, with ground-truth trajectories for five driving styles and natural-language instructions. Experiments show that our 4B-parameter StyleVLA significantly outperforms proprietary models (e.g., Gemini-3-Pro) and state-of-the-art VLA models. Using a composite driving score measuring success rate, physical feasibility, and style adherence, StyleVLA achieves 0.55 on BEV and 0.51 on FPV, versus 0.32 and 0.35 for Gemini-3-Pro. These results show that a specialized, physics-informed, lightweight model can surpass closed-source models on domain-specific tasks.

摘要（中文）: 视觉语言模型（VLM）连接了视觉感知和语言推理。在自动驾驶（AD）领域，这种协同作用促成了视觉语言行动（VLA）模型的诞生，该模型将高层次的多模态理解转化为驾驶行为，通常表示为未来轨迹。然而，现有的VLA模型主要生成通用的、无碰撞轨迹。除了避免碰撞之外，适应不同的驾驶风格（例如，运动型、舒适型）对于个性化驾驶至关重要。此外，许多方法将轨迹生成视为简单的token预测，这可能导致运动学上不可行的动作。为了解决这些局限性，我们提出了StyleVLA，一个物理信息驱动的VLA框架，用于生成多样且物理上合理的驾驶行为。我们引入了一种混合损失，它将运动学一致性约束与连续回归头结合起来，以提高轨迹的可行性。为了训练建立在Qwen3-VL-4B上的StyleVLA，我们构建了一个大规模指令数据集，包含超过1.2k个场景，76k个鸟瞰图（BEV）样本和42k个第一人称视角（FPV）样本，以及五种驾驶风格的真实轨迹和自然语言指令。实验表明，我们40亿参数的StyleVLA显著优于专有模型（例如，Gemini-3-Pro）和最先进的VLA模型。使用一个综合驾驶评分来衡量成功率、物理可行性和风格一致性，StyleVLA在BEV上实现了0.55分，在FPV上实现了0.51分，而Gemini-3-Pro分别为0.32分和0.35分。这些结果表明，一个专门的、物理信息驱动的、轻量级模型可以在特定领域的任务上超越闭源模型。

TLDR: StyleVLA introduces a physics-informed VLA framework, trained on a new dataset, to generate diverse and physically plausible driving behaviors conditioned on driving style and natural language instructions, outperforming existing and proprietary models.

简要说明: StyleVLA 提出了一个受物理信息约束的 VLA 框架，该框架在一个新的数据集上训练，可以根据驾驶风格和自然语言指令生成多样化且物理上合理的驾驶行为，并且优于现有模型和专有模型。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yuan Gao, Dengyuan Hua, Mattia Piccinini, Finn Rasmus Schäfer, Korbinian Moller, Lin Li, Johannes Betz

SEA-Nav: Efficient Policy Learning for Safe and Agile Quadruped Navigation in Cluttered Environments

Abstract (English): Efficiently training quadruped robot navigation in densely cluttered environments remains a significant challenge. Existing methods are either limited by a lack of safety and agility in simple obstacle distributions or suffer from slow locomotion in complex environments, often requiring excessively long training phases. To this end, we propose SEA-Nav (Safe, Efficient, and Agile Navigation), a reinforcement learning framework for quadruped navigation. Within diverse and dense obstacle environments, a differentiable control barrier function (CBF)-based shield constraints the navigation policy to output safe velocity commands. An adaptive collision replay mechanism and hazardous exploration rewards are introduced to increase the probability of learning from critical experiences, guiding efficient exploration and exploitation. Finally, kinematic action constraints are incorporated to ensure safe velocity commands, facilitating successful physical deployment. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first approach that achieves highly challenging quadruped navigation in the real world with minute-level training time.

摘要（中文）: 在密集杂乱环境中高效训练四足机器人导航仍然是一个重大挑战。现有方法要么受限于在简单障碍物分布中的安全性和敏捷性不足，要么在复杂环境中遭受缓慢的运动速度，并且通常需要过长的训练阶段。为此，我们提出SEA-Nav（安全、高效和敏捷导航），一种用于四足机器人导航的强化学习框架。在多样且密集的障碍物环境中，基于可微控制屏障函数（CBF）的盾状约束限制导航策略输出安全的速度指令。引入自适应碰撞回放机制和危险探索奖励，以增加从关键经验中学习的概率，从而引导高效的探索和利用。最后，结合运动学动作约束，确保安全的速度指令，促进成功的物理部署。据我们所知，这是第一个在现实世界中以分钟级别的训练时间实现极具挑战性的四足机器人导航的方法。

TLDR: SEA-Nav is a reinforcement learning framework for quadruped navigation in cluttered environments, utilizing a CBF-based shield, adaptive collision replay, hazardous exploration rewards, and kinematic action constraints to achieve safe and agile navigation with minimal training time.

简要说明: SEA-Nav是一个用于复杂环境中四足机器人导航的强化学习框架，它利用基于 CBF 的安全屏障、自适应碰撞回放、危险探索奖励和运动学动作约束，以极短的训练时间实现安全和敏捷的导航。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Shiyi Chen, Mingye Yang, Haiyan Mao, Jiaqi Zhang, Haiyi Liu, Shuheng He, Debing Zhang, Zihao Qiu, Chun Zhang

Open-World Motion Forecasting

Abstract (English): Motion forecasting aims to predict the future trajectories of dynamic agents in the scene, enabling autonomous vehicles to effectively reason about scene evolution. Existing approaches operate under the closed-world regime and assume fixed object taxonomy as well as access to high-quality perception. Therefore, they struggle in real-world settings where perception is imperfect and object taxonomy evolves over time. In this work, we bridge this fundamental gap by introducing open-world motion forecasting, a novel setting in which new object classes are sequentially introduced over time and future object trajectories are estimated directly from camera images. We tackle this setting by proposing the first end-to-end class-incremental motion forecasting framework to mitigate catastrophic forgetting while simultaneously learning to forecast newly introduced classes. When a new class is introduced, our framework employs a pseudo-labeling strategy to first generate motion forecasting pseudo-labels for all known classes which are then processed by a vision-language model to filter inconsistent and over-confident predictions. Parallelly, our approach further mitigates catastrophic forgetting by using a novel replay sampling strategy that leverages query feature variance to sample previous sequences with informative motion patterns. Extensive evaluation on the nuScenes and Argoverse 2 datasets demonstrates that our approach successfully resists catastrophic forgetting and maintains performance on previously learned classes while improving adaptation to novel ones. Further, we demonstrate that our approach supports zero-shot transfer to real-world driving and naturally extends to end-to-end class-incremental planning, enabling continual adaptation of the full autonomous driving system. We provide the code at https://omen.cs.uni-freiburg.de .

摘要（中文）: 运动预测旨在预测场景中动态智能体的未来轨迹，使自动驾驶车辆能够有效地推断场景演变。现有方法在封闭世界的范畴内运行，并假设固定的对象分类以及对高质量感知的获取。因此，它们在感知不完善且对象分类随时间演变的真实世界环境中表现不佳。在本文中，我们通过引入开放世界运动预测来弥合这一根本差距，这是一种全新的设置，其中新的对象类别随时间依次引入，并且直接从相机图像估计未来的对象轨迹。我们通过提出首个端到端的类增量运动预测框架来解决此问题，该框架旨在缓解灾难性遗忘，同时学习预测新引入的类别。当引入新的类别时，我们的框架采用伪标记策略，首先为所有已知类别生成运动预测伪标签，然后由视觉-语言模型处理这些伪标签，以过滤不一致和过度自信的预测。与此同时，我们的方法通过使用一种新颖的重放抽样策略来进一步缓解灾难性遗忘，该策略利用查询特征方差来抽样具有信息量运动模式的先前序列。在nuScenes和Argoverse 2数据集上的大量评估表明，我们的方法成功地抵抗了灾难性遗忘，并在先前学习的类别上保持了性能，同时提高了对新类别的适应性。此外，我们证明了我们的方法支持零样本迁移到真实世界驾驶，并自然地扩展到端到端的类增量规划，从而实现完整自动驾驶系统的持续适应。 我们的代码在https://omen.cs.uni-freiburg.de 提供。

TLDR: This paper introduces "open-world motion forecasting," addresses the challenge of continual learning in motion forecasting where new object classes are constantly introduced, and proposes a class-incremental learning framework using pseudo-labeling and a replay sampling strategy.

简要说明: 本文介绍了“开放世界运动预测”，解决了运动预测中不断引入新对象类别的持续学习挑战，并提出了一个使用伪标记和重放采样策略的类增量学习框架。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Nicolas Schischka, Nikhil Gosala, B Ravi Kiran, Senthil Yogamani, Abhinav Valada

From Flow to One Step: Real-Time Multi-Modal Trajectory Policies via Implicit Maximum Likelihood Estimation-based Distribution Distillation

Abstract (English): Generative policies based on diffusion and flow matching achieve strong performance in robotic manipulation by modeling multi-modal human demonstrations. However, their reliance on iterative Ordinary Differential Equation (ODE) integration introduces substantial latency, limiting high-frequency closed-loop control. Recent single-step acceleration methods alleviate this overhead but often exhibit distributional collapse, producing averaged trajectories that fail to execute coherent manipulation strategies. We propose a framework that distills a Conditional Flow Matching (CFM) expert into a fast single-step student via Implicit Maximum Likelihood Estimation (IMLE). A bi-directional Chamfer distance provides a set-level objective that promotes both mode coverage and fidelity, enabling preservation of the teacher multi-modal action distribution in a single forward pass. A unified perception encoder further integrates multi-view RGB, depth, point clouds, and proprioception into a geometry-aware representation. The resulting high-frequency control supports real-time receding-horizon re-planning and improved robustness under dynamic disturbances.

摘要（中文）: 基于扩散模型和流匹配的生成策略通过建模多模态人类示教，在机器人操作中取得了优异的性能。然而，它们对迭代式常微分方程(ODE)积分的依赖引入了显著的延迟，限制了高频闭环控制。最近的单步加速方法减轻了这种开销，但通常会表现出分布坍塌，产生平均化的轨迹，无法执行连贯的操作策略。我们提出了一种框架，通过隐式最大似然估计(IMLE)将条件流匹配(CFM)专家提炼成快速单步学生模型。一种双向Chamfer距离提供了一个集合级别目标，既促进了模式覆盖，又保证了保真度，从而能够在单次前向传播中保留教师模型的多模态动作分布。一个统一的感知编码器进一步将多视角RGB、深度、点云和本体感觉信息整合到一个几何感知的表示中。由此产生的高频控制支持实时滚动时域重规划，并提高了在动态扰动下的鲁棒性。

TLDR: This paper proposes a framework for distilling a flow-matching-based generative policy into a fast, single-step policy using Implicit Maximum Likelihood Estimation to enable real-time robotic manipulation with multi-modal demonstrations, addressing the latency issues of flow-based methods and distributional collapse of single-step methods.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种框架，通过隐式最大似然估计将基于流匹配的生成策略提炼成快速的单步策略，从而实现具有多模态演示的实时机器人操作。该方法解决了基于流的方法的延迟问题以及单步方法中的分布崩溃问题。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Ju Dong, Liding Zhang, Lei Zhang, Yu Fu, Kaixin Bai, Zoltan-Csaba Marton, Zhenshan Bing, Zhaopeng Chen, Alois Christian Knoll, Jianwei Zhang

SPAARS: Safer RL Policy Alignment through Abstract Exploration and Refined Exploitation of Action Space

Abstract (English): Offline-to-online reinforcement learning (RL) offers a promising paradigm for robotics by pre-training policies on safe, offline demonstrations and fine-tuning them via online interaction. However, a fundamental challenge remains: how to safely explore online without deviating from the behavioral support of the offline data? While recent methods leverage conditional variational autoencoders (CVAEs) to bound exploration within a latent space, they inherently suffer from an exploitation gap -- a performance ceiling imposed by the decoder's reconstruction loss. We introduce SPAARS, a curriculum learning framework that initially constrains exploration to the low-dimensional latent manifold for sample-efficient, safe behavioral improvement, then seamlessly transfers control to the raw action space, bypassing the decoder bottleneck. SPAARS has two instantiations: the CVAE-based variant requires only unordered (s,a) pairs and no trajectory segmentation; SPAARS-SUPE pairs SPAARS with OPAL temporal skill pretraining for stronger exploration structure at the cost of requiring trajectory chunks. We prove an upper bound on the exploitation gap using the Performance Difference Lemma, establish that latent-space policy gradients achieve provable variance reduction over raw-space exploration, and show that concurrent behavioral cloning during the latent phase directly controls curriculum transition stability. Empirically, SPAARS-SUPE achieves 0.825 normalized return on kitchen-mixed-v0 versus 0.75 for SUPE, with 5x better sample efficiency; standalone SPAARS achieves 92.7 and 102.9 normalized return on hopper-medium-v2 and walker2d-medium-v2 respectively, surpassing IQL baselines of 66.3 and 78.3 respectively, confirming the utility of the unordered-pair CVAE instantiation.

摘要（中文）: 离线到在线强化学习（RL）为机器人技术提供了一种有前景的范式，它通过在安全的离线演示数据上预训练策略，并通过在线交互进行微调。然而，一个根本性的挑战仍然存在：如何在不偏离离线数据的行为支持的情况下安全地进行在线探索？虽然最近的方法利用条件变分自编码器（CVAE）将探索限制在潜在空间内，但它们固有地遭受利用率差距——一个由解码器的重构损失所设定的性能上限。我们引入了SPAARS，一个课程学习框架，它最初将探索约束到低维潜在流形，以实现样本高效、安全的行为改进，然后无缝地将控制权转移到原始动作空间，绕过了解码器瓶颈。SPAARS有两种实例化：基于CVAE的变体只需要无序的(s,a)对，而不需要轨迹分割；SPAARS-SUPE将SPAARS与OPAL时间技能预训练配对，以获得更强的探索结构，但需要轨迹块为代价。我们利用性能差异引理证明了利用率差距的上界，证明了潜在空间策略梯度实现了可证明的方差降低优于原始空间探索，并表明潜在阶段的并发行为克隆直接控制课程过渡的稳定性。在实验中，SPAARS-SUPE在kitchen-mixed-v0上实现了0.825的归一化回报，而SUPE为0.75，样本效率提高了5倍；独立的SPAARS在hopper-medium-v2和walker2d-medium-v2上分别实现了92.7和102.9的归一化回报，超过了IQL基线的66.3和78.3，证实了无序对CVAE实例化的效用。

TLDR: This paper introduces SPAARS, a curriculum learning framework for safe offline-to-online RL that improves sample efficiency and safety in robotics by initially exploring a latent space and then transitioning to the raw action space, outperforming existing methods on benchmark tasks.

简要说明: 本文介绍了SPAARS，一个用于安全离线到在线强化学习的课程学习框架，通过先在潜在空间探索再过渡到原始动作空间，提高了机器人技术的样本效率和安全性，并在基准任务上优于现有方法。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Swaminathan S K, Aritra Hazra

CORAL: Scalable Multi-Task Robot Learning via LoRA Experts

Abstract (English): Deploying Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models in real-world robotics exposes a core multi-task learning challenge: reconciling task interference in multi-task robotic learning. When multiple tasks are jointly fine-tuned in a single stage, gradients from different tasks can conflict, causing negative transfer and reducing per-task performance. Yet maintaining a separate full checkpoint per task is often storage- and deployment-prohibitive. To address this dilemma, we present CORAL, a backbone- and embodiment-agnostic framework designed primarily to mitigate multi-task interference while remaining naturally extensible to a continuous stream of new tasks. CORAL freezes a single pre-trained VLA backbone and attaches one lightweight Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) expert per task; at runtime, a dynamic inference engine (the CORAL Manager) routes language instructions to the appropriate expert and swaps experts on the fly with zero inference overhead. This strict parameter isolation avoids complex gating networks and prevents parameter-level cross-task interference by construction; as an added capability, it also enables sequentially introducing new tasks without parameter overwriting caused by catastrophic forgetting. We validate CORAL on a real-world Galaxea R1 dual-arm mobile manipulator and three simulation benchmarks (LIBERO, WidowX, Google Robot), where CORAL overcomes fine-grained instructional ambiguity and substantially outperforms joint training, yielding a practical and scalable system for lifelong multi-task robot learning. Website: https://frontierrobo.github.io/CORAL

摘要（中文）: 在真实世界机器人技术中部署视觉-语言-动作 (VLA) 模型面临着一个核心的多任务学习挑战：调和多任务机器人学习中的任务干涉。当多个任务在单一阶段联合微调时，来自不同任务的梯度可能冲突，导致负迁移并降低每个任务的性能。然而，为每个任务维护一个单独的完整检查点通常会带来存储和部署的限制。为了解决这一难题，我们提出了 CORAL，一种与骨干网络和具身性无关的框架，其主要设计目的是缓解多任务干涉，同时保持对持续新增任务的自然可扩展性。CORAL 冻结了一个预训练的 VLA 骨干网络，并为每个任务附加一个轻量级的低秩适应 (LoRA) 专家；在运行时，一个动态推理引擎（CORAL 管理器）将语言指令路由到相应的专家，并实时切换专家，且无推理开销。这种严格的参数隔离避免了复杂的门控网络，并通过构建防止了参数级的跨任务干涉；作为一项附加能力，它还支持顺序引入新任务，避免了灾难性遗忘导致的参数覆盖。我们在真实世界的 Galaxea R1 双臂移动机械臂和三个仿真基准 (LIBERO, WidowX, Google Robot) 上验证了 CORAL，结果表明 CORAL 克服了细粒度的指令模糊性，并显著优于联合训练，从而产生了一个实用且可扩展的终身多任务机器人学习系统。网站：https://frontierrobo.github.io/CORAL

TLDR: The paper introduces CORAL, a scalable multi-task robot learning framework using LoRA experts to mitigate task interference in VLA models while enabling lifelong learning. It achieves superior performance compared to joint training across several robotics benchmarks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了CORAL，一个可扩展的多任务机器人学习框架，它使用LoRA专家来缓解VLA模型中的任务干扰，同时实现终身学习。它在多个机器人基准测试中实现了优于联合训练的性能。

Authors: Yuankai Luo, Woping Chen, Tong Liang, Zhenguo Li

RAE-NWM: Navigation World Model in Dense Visual Representation Space

Abstract (English): Visual navigation requires agents to reach goals in complex environments through perception and planning. World models address this task by simulating action-conditioned state transitions to predict future observations. Current navigation world models typically learn state evolution under actions within the compressed latent space of a Variational Autoencoder, where spatial compression often discards fine-grained structural information and hinders precise control. To better understand the propagation characteristics of different representations, we conduct a linear dynamics probe and observe that dense DINOv2 features exhibit stronger linear predictability for action-conditioned transitions. Motivated by this observation, we propose the Representation Autoencoder-based Navigation World Model (RAE-NWM), which models navigation dynamics in a dense visual representation space. We employ a Conditional Diffusion Transformer with Decoupled Diffusion Transformer head (CDiT-DH) to model continuous transitions, and introduce a separate time-driven gating module for dynamics conditioning to regulate action injection strength during generation. Extensive evaluations show that modeling sequential rollouts in this space improves structural stability and action accuracy, benefiting downstream planning and navigation.

摘要（中文）: 基于表征自编码器的导航世界模型 视觉导航需要智能体通过感知和规划来达到复杂环境中的目标。世界模型通过模拟以动作为条件的state transitions来预测未来观测，从而解决这一问题。目前的导航世界模型通常在变分自编码器的压缩潜在空间中学习动作下的状态演化，其中空间压缩常常丢弃细粒度的结构信息，阻碍精确控制。为了更好地理解不同表征的传播特性，我们进行了线性动力学探测，并观察到密集的DINOv2特征在动作条件下的状态转移表现出更强的线性可预测性。受到这一观察的启发，我们提出了基于表征自编码器的导航世界模型（RAE-NWM），它在密集的视觉表征空间中对导航动力学进行建模。我们采用具有解耦扩散Transformer头的条件扩散Transformer（CDiT-DH）来对连续状态转移进行建模，并引入一个独立的时间驱动门控模块用于动力学调节，以控制生成过程中动作注入的强度。大量评估表明，在该空间中对顺序推演进行建模可以提高结构稳定性和动作准确性，从而有利于下游的规划和导航。

TLDR: The paper introduces RAE-NWM, a navigation world model that operates in a dense visual representation space using DINOv2 features and a Conditional Diffusion Transformer, demonstrating improved structural stability and action accuracy for visual navigation.

简要说明: 该论文提出了RAE-NWM，一种在密集视觉表征空间中运行的导航世界模型，它使用DINOv2特征和条件扩散Transformer， 从而提高了视觉导航的结构稳定性和动作准确性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)

Authors: Mingkun Zhang, Wangtian Shen, Fan Zhang, Haijian Qin, Zihao Pei, Ziyang Meng

MO-Playground: Massively Parallelized Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning for Robotics

Abstract (English): Multi-objective reinforcement learning (MORL) is a powerful tool to learn Pareto-optimal policy families across conflicting objectives. However, unlike traditional RL algorithms, existing MORL algorithms do not effectively leverage large-scale parallelization to concurrently simulate thousands of environments, resulting in vastly increased computation time. Ultimately, this has limited MORL's application towards complex multi-objective robotics problems. To address these challenges, we present 1) MORLAX, a new GPU-native, fast MORL algorithm, and 2) MO-Playground, a pip-installable playground of GPU-accelerated multi-objective environments. Together, MORLAX and MO-Playground approximate Pareto sets within minutes, offering 25-270x speed-ups compared to legacy CPU-based approaches whilst achieving superior Pareto front hypervolumes. We demonstrate the versatility of our approach by implementing a custom BRUCE humanoid robot environment using MO-Playground and learning Pareto-optimal locomotion policies across 6 realistic objectives for BRUCE, such as smoothness, efficiency and arm swinging.

摘要（中文）: 多目标强化学习 (MORL) 是一种强大的工具，用于学习跨冲突目标的 Pareto 最优策略族。然而，与传统的强化学习算法不同，现有的 MORL 算法不能有效地利用大规模并行化来同时模拟数千个环境，导致计算时间大幅增加。最终，这限制了 MORL 在复杂多目标机器人问题上的应用。为了解决这些挑战，我们提出了 1) MORLAX，一种新的 GPU 原生、快速的 MORL 算法，以及 2) MO-Playground，一个可通过 pip 安装的 GPU 加速多目标环境试验场。MORLAX 和 MO-Playground 共同在几分钟内逼近 Pareto 集，与传统的基于 CPU 的方法相比，速度提高了 25-270 倍，同时实现了更高的 Pareto 前沿超体积。我们通过使用 MO-Playground 实现一个自定义的 BRUCE 类人机器人环境，并学习 BRUCE 在 6 个现实目标（例如平滑度、效率和手臂摆动）下的 Pareto 最优运动策略，来展示我们方法的通用性。

TLDR: This paper introduces MORLAX, a GPU-accelerated MORL algorithm, and MO-Playground, a platform for multi-objective robotics environments, achieving significant speedups and improved Pareto front hypervolumes compared to CPU-based approaches, demonstrated on a humanoid robot.

简要说明: 本文介绍了MORLAX，一种GPU加速的多目标强化学习算法，以及MO-Playground，一个用于多目标机器人环境的平台。与基于CPU的方法相比，该方法实现了显著的加速并改善了Pareto前沿的超体积，并在一个人形机器人上进行了演示。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Neil Janwani, Ellen Novoseller, Vernon J. Lawhern, Maegan Tucker

STONE Dataset: A Scalable Multi-Modal Surround-View 3D Traversability Dataset for Off-Road Robot Navigation

Abstract (English): Reliable off-road navigation requires accurate estimation of traversable regions and robust perception under diverse terrain and sensing conditions. However, existing datasets lack both scalability and multi-modality, which limits progress in 3D traversability prediction. In this work, we introduce STONE, a large-scale multi-modal dataset for off-road navigation. STONE provides (1) trajectory-guided 3D traversability maps generated by a fully automated, annotation-free pipeline, and (2) comprehensive surround-view sensing with synchronized 128-channel LiDAR, six RGB cameras, and three 4D imaging radars. The dataset covers a wide range of environments and conditions, including day and night, grasslands, farmlands, construction sites, and lakes. Our auto-labeling pipeline reconstructs dense terrain surfaces from LiDAR scans, extracts geometric attributes such as slope, elevation, and roughness, and assigns traversability labels beyond the robot's trajectory using a Mahalanobis-distance-based criterion. This design enables scalable, geometry-aware ground-truth construction without manual annotation. Finally, we establish a benchmark for voxel-level 3D traversability prediction and provide strong baselines under both single-modal and multi-modal settings. STONE is available at: https://konyul.github.io/STONE-dataset/

摘要（中文）: 可靠的越野导航需要在不同的地形和感知条件下准确估计可通行区域并实现稳健的感知。然而，现有数据集缺乏可扩展性和多模态，这限制了三维可通行性预测的进展。在这项工作中，我们提出了STONE，一个用于越野导航的大规模多模态数据集。STONE提供：（1）由全自动、无标注流程生成的轨迹引导的三维可通行性地图，以及（2）全面的环视感知，同步包括128线激光雷达、六个RGB相机和三个4D成像雷达。该数据集涵盖了广泛的环境和条件，包括白天和夜晚、草地、农田、建筑工地和湖泊。我们的自动标注流程从激光雷达扫描重建密集地形表面，提取几何属性，如坡度、海拔和粗糙度，并使用基于马氏距离的标准，在机器人轨迹之外分配可通行性标签。这种设计无需手动标注即可实现可扩展的、几何感知的基础真值构建。最后，我们建立了体素级三维可通行性预测的基准，并在单模态和多模态设置下提供了强大的基线模型。STONE可在以下网址获取：https://konyul.github.io/STONE-dataset/

TLDR: The paper introduces STONE, a large-scale, multi-modal dataset for off-road robot navigation featuring trajectory-guided 3D traversability maps and comprehensive surround-view sensing, with an automated annotation pipeline and baseline benchmarks for 3D traversability prediction.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了STONE，一个用于越野机器人导航的大规模多模态数据集，具有轨迹引导的三维可通行性地图和全面的环视感知系统，配备自动标注流程以及三维可通行性预测的基准。

Novelty: (7/10)

Authors: Konyul Park, Daehun Kim, Jiyong Oh, Seunghoon Yu, Junseo Park, Jaehyun Park, Hongjae Shin, Hyungchan Cho, Jungho Kim, Jun Won Choi

ZeroWBC: Learning Natural Visuomotor Humanoid Control Directly from Human Egocentric Video

Abstract (English): Achieving versatile and naturalistic whole-body control for humanoid robot scene-interaction remains a significant challenge. While some recent works have demonstrated autonomous humanoid interactive control, they are constrained to rigid locomotion patterns and expensive teleoperation data collection, lacking the versatility to execute more human-like natural behaviors such as sitting or kicking. Furthermore, acquiring the necessary real robot teleoperation data is prohibitively expensive and time-consuming. To address these limitations, we introduce ZeroWBC, a novel framework that learns a natural humanoid visuomotor control policy directly from human egocentric videos, eliminating the need for large-scale robot teleoperation data and enabling natural humanoid robot scene-interaction control. Specifically, our approach first fine-tunes a Vision-Language Model (VLM) to predict future whole-body human motions based on text instructions and egocentric visual context, then these generated motions are retargeted to real robot joints and executed via our robust general motion tracking policy for humanoid whole-body control. Extensive experiments on the Unitree G1 humanoid robot demonstrate that our method outperforms baseline approaches in motion naturalness and versatility, successfully establishing a pipeline that eliminates teleoperation data collection overhead for whole-body humanoid control, offering a scalable and efficient paradigm for general humanoid whole-body control.

摘要（中文）: 实现通用且自然的类人机器人全身控制，以进行场景交互，仍然是一项重大挑战。虽然最近的一些工作已经展示了自主类人机器人交互控制，但它们受限于刚性的运动模式和昂贵的遥操作数据采集，缺乏执行更像人类的自然行为（如坐下或踢腿）的通用性。此外，获取必要的真实机器人遥操作数据成本高昂且耗时。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了ZeroWBC，一种新颖的框架，可以直接从人类主视角视频中学习自然的类人机器人视觉运动控制策略，无需大规模机器人遥操作数据，并实现自然的类人机器人场景交互控制。具体来说，我们的方法首先微调一个视觉-语言模型（VLM），以根据文本指令和主视角视觉上下文预测未来的全身人体运动，然后将这些生成的运动重定向到真实的机器人关节，并通过我们鲁棒的通用运动跟踪策略执行，从而实现类人机器人的全身控制。在宇树G1类人机器人上的大量实验表明，我们的方法在运动自然性和通用性方面优于基线方法，成功建立了一个Eliminating Teleoperation Data Collection Overhead（消除遥操作数据采集开销）的管线来进行全身类人机器人控制，为通用类人机器人全身控制提供了一个可扩展且高效的范例。

TLDR: ZeroWBC learns natural humanoid control from human egocentric videos by fine-tuning a VLM to predict human motions, which are then retargeted to a robot, eliminating the need for expensive teleoperation data.

简要说明: ZeroWBC通过从人类第一视角视频中学习，微调视觉-语言模型以预测人体运动，然后将这些运动重定向到机器人，从而实现了自然的人形机器人控制，并消除了昂贵的远程操作数据的需求。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Haoran Yang, Jiacheng Bao, Yucheng Xin, Haoming Song, Yuyang Tian, Bin Zhao, Dong Wang, Xuelong Li

DexHiL: A Human-in-the-Loop Framework for Vision-Language-Action Model Post-Training in Dexterous Manipulation

Abstract (English): While Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have demonstrated promising generalization capabilities in robotic manipulation, deploying them on specific and complex downstream tasks still demands effective post-training. In parallel, Human-in-the-Loop (HiL) learning has proven to be a powerful mechanism for refining robot policies. However, extending this paradigm to dexterous manipulation remains challenging: multi-finger control is high-dimensional, contact-intensive, and exhibits execution distributions that differ markedly from standard arm motions, leaving existing dexterous VLA systems limited in reliability and adaptability. We present DexHiL, the first integrated arm-hand human-in-the-loop framework for dexterous VLA models, enabling coordinated interventions over the arm and the dexterous hand within a single system. DexHiL introduces an intervention-aware data sampling strategy that prioritizes corrective segments for post-training, alongside a lightweight teleoperation interface that supports instantaneous human corrections during execution. Real-robot experiments demonstrate that DexHiL serves as an effective post-training framework, yielding a substantial performance leap, outperforming standard offline-only fine-tuning baselines by an average of 25% in success rates across distinct tasks. Project page: https://chenzhongxi-sjtu.github.io/dexhil/

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型在机器人操控中已展现出令人鼓舞的泛化能力，但将其部署到特定且复杂的下游任务仍需要有效的后训练。与此同时，人机协作（HiL）学习已被证明是改进机器人策略的强大机制。然而，将这种范式扩展到灵巧操控仍然具有挑战性：多指控制是高维的、接触密集的，并且表现出与标准手臂运动明显不同的执行分布，使得现有的灵巧VLA系统在可靠性和适应性方面存在局限性。我们提出了DexHiL，这是第一个集成手臂-手的灵巧VLA模型人机协作框架，能够在单个系统中对机械臂和灵巧手进行协调干预。 DexHiL引入了一种感知干预的数据采样策略，该策略优先考虑用于后训练的修正片段，同时提供了一个轻量级的远程操作界面，支持在执行过程中进行即时的人工修正。真实机器人实验表明，DexHiL作为一个有效的后训练框架，可以带来显著的性能提升，在不同任务中的成功率平均比标准的纯离线微调基线高出25%。 项目主页：https://chenzhongxi-sjtu.github.io/dexhil/

TLDR: The paper introduces DexHiL, a human-in-the-loop framework for post-training vision-language-action models specifically designed for dexterous robotic manipulation. It demonstrates significant performance improvements over offline fine-tuning baselines.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种名为 DexHiL 的人机协同框架，用于对视觉-语言-动作模型进行后训练，专门用于灵巧机器人操作。实验表明，与离线微调基线相比，该框架实现了显著的性能提升。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)

Authors: Yifan Han, Zhongxi Chen, Yuxuan Zhao, Congsheng Xu, Yanming Shao, Yichuan Peng, Yao Mu, Wenzhao Lian

PM-Nav: Priori-Map Guided Embodied Navigation in Functional Buildings

Abstract (English): Existing language-driven embodied navigation paradigms face challenges in functional buildings (FBs) with highly similar features, as they lack the ability to effectively utilize priori spatial knowledge. To tackle this issue, we propose a Priori-Map Guided Embodied Navigation (PM-Nav), wherein environmental maps are transformed into navigation-friendly semantic priori-maps, a hierarchical chain-of-thought prompt template with an annotation priori-map is designed to enable precise path planning, and a multi-model collaborative action output mechanism is built to accomplish positioning decisions and execution control for navigation planning. Comprehensive tests using a home-made FB dataset show that the PM-Nav obtains average improvements of 511\% and 1175\%, and 650\% and 400\% over the SG-Nav and the InstructNav in simulation and real-world, respectively. These tremendous boosts elucidate the great potential of using the PM-Nav as a backbone navigation framework for FBs.

摘要（中文）: 现有语言驱动的具身导航范式在特征高度相似的功能性建筑（FBs）中面临挑战，因为它们缺乏有效利用先验空间知识的能力。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种先验地图引导的具身导航（PM-Nav），其中环境地图被转换为导航友好的语义先验地图，设计了一种带有注释先验地图的层次化思维链提示模板以实现精确的路径规划，并构建了一种多模型协同动作输出机制，以完成导航规划的定位决策和执行控制。使用自制FB数据集进行的全面测试表明，在模拟和真实世界中，PM-Nav的性能分别比SG-Nav提高了511%和1175%，比InstructNav提高了650%和400%。这些巨大的提升阐明了PM-Nav作为功能性建筑骨干导航框架的巨大潜力。

TLDR: The paper introduces PM-Nav, a navigation framework for functional buildings that uses semantic priori-maps and a hierarchical chain-of-thought prompting approach, demonstrating significant performance improvements over existing methods in both simulation and real-world environments.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种名为PM-Nav的导航框架，用于功能性建筑。该框架利用语义先验地图和分层链式思考提示方法，在模拟和真实环境中都表现出比现有方法显著的性能提升。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)

Authors: Jiang Gao, Xiangyu Dong, Haozhou Li, Haoran Zhao, Yaoming Zhou, Xiaoguang Ma

Provably Safe Trajectory Generation for Manipulators Under Motion and Environmental Uncertainties

Abstract (English): Robot manipulators operating in uncertain and non-convex environments present significant challenges for safe and optimal motion planning. Existing methods often struggle to provide efficient and formally certified collision risk guarantees, particularly when dealing with complex geometries and non-Gaussian uncertainties. This article proposes a novel risk-bounded motion planning framework to address this unmet need. Our approach integrates a rigid manipulator deep stochastic Koopman operator (RM-DeSKO) model to robustly predict the robot's state distribution under motion uncertainty. We then introduce an efficient, hierarchical verification method that combines parallelizable physics simulations with sum-of-squares (SOS) programming as a filter for fine-grained, formal certification of collision risk. This method is embedded within a Model Predictive Path Integral (MPPI) controller that uniquely utilizes binary collision information from SOS decomposition to improve its policy. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated on two typical robot manipulators through extensive simulations and real-world experiments, including a challenging human-robot collaboration scenario, demonstrating sim-to-real transfer of the learned model and its ability to generate safe and efficient trajectories in complex, uncertain settings.

摘要（中文）: 在不确定和非凸环境中运行的机器人机械臂为安全和最优的运动规划带来了重大挑战。现有方法通常难以提供高效且经过形式化验证的碰撞风险保障，尤其是在处理复杂几何形状和非高斯不确定性时。本文提出了一种新型的风险限定运动规划框架，以解决这一未被满足的需求。我们的方法集成了一个刚性机械臂深度随机Koopman算子（RM-DeSKO）模型，以鲁棒地预测运动不确定性下机器人的状态分布。然后，我们引入一种高效的、分层的验证方法，该方法将可并行化的物理仿真与平方和（SOS）规划相结合，作为对碰撞风险进行细粒度、形式化认证的过滤器。该方法嵌入在模型预测路径积分（MPPI）控制器中，后者独特地利用来自SOS分解的二元碰撞信息来改进其策略。通过大量的仿真和真实世界的实验，包括具有挑战性的人机协作场景，验证了所提出框架的有效性。结果表明，所学习的模型能够实现从仿真到真实的迁移，并在复杂、不确定的环境中生成安全高效的轨迹。

TLDR: This paper presents a risk-bounded motion planning framework for robot manipulators in uncertain environments, using a deep stochastic Koopman operator and a hierarchical verification method for formally certified collision risk guarantees, validated through simulations and real-world experiments.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一种用于在不确定环境中机器人机械臂的风险约束运动规划框架，该框架使用深度随机Koopman算子和分层验证方法，以获得正式认证的碰撞风险保证，并通过仿真和实际实验验证。

Relevance: (7/10)
