Authors: Fei Meng, Zijiang Yang, Xinyu Mao, Haobo Liang, Max Q. -H. Meng

GST-VLA: Structured Gaussian Spatial Tokens for 3D Depth-Aware Vision-Language-Action Models

Abstract (English): VLA models encode visual observations as 2D patch tokens with no intrinsic geometric structure. We introduce GST-VLA with two contributions. First, the Gaussian Spatial Tokenizer (GST) converts frozen dense depth and frozen semantic patch features into $N_g{=}128$ anisotropic 3D Gaussian primitives, each parameterized by a metric residual mean $μ\in \mathbb{R}^3$, log-scale covariance $\log σ\in \mathbb{R}^3$, and learned opacity $α\in (0,1)$. The covariance eigenstructure encodes local surface orientation, and opacity provides per-primitive geometric confidence, both inaccessible from scalar depth. Spatial attention pooling with learned queries concentrates the fixed token budget on geometrically salient regions rather than distributing uniformly. Second, 3D Depth-Aware Chain-of-Thought (DA-CoT) reasoning supervises four structured intermediate spatial thoughts, covering 3D object grounding, grasp affordance contact geometry, pairwise metric distances, and coarse SE(3) waypoints, as explicit generation targets in the training loss. A cross-attention sublayer at every VLM transformer block provides direct access to the raw 256-primitive Gaussian field during DA-CoT generation. A 300M-parameter flow-matching action expert with mixture-of-experts feedforward sublayers decodes 7-DoF delta action chunks via conditional ODE integration, conditioned on both VLM hidden states and DA-CoT outputs through dual cross-attention. Trained with composite $\mathcal{L}_\mathrm{flow} + \mathcal{L}_\mathrm{CoT} + \mathcal{L}_\mathrm{depth}$ across three progressive stages, GST-VLA achieves 96.4% on LIBERO (+2.0%), and 80.2% on SimplerEnv (+5.4%). Ablations isolate the contribution of each GST component, each DA-CoT thought, and each training stage, confirming independent and synergistic gains concentrated on precision demanding tasks.

摘要（中文）: VLA模型将视觉观测编码为缺乏内在几何结构的2D图像块令牌。我们引入 GST-VLA，并提出两项贡献。首先，高斯空间令牌器 (GST) 将冻结的稠密深度和冻结的语义图像块特征转换为 $N_g{=}128$ 个各向异性 3D 高斯基元，每个基元都由一个度量残差均值 $μ\in \mathbb{R}^3$、对数尺度协方差 $\log σ\in \mathbb{R}^3$ 和学习的透明度 $α\in (0,1)$ 参数化。协方差特征结构编码局部表面方向，而透明度提供每个基元的几何置信度，这两者都无法从标量深度中获得。通过学习的查询进行的空间注意力池化将固定的令牌预算集中在几何上显著的区域，而不是均匀分布。其次，3D 深度感知思维链 (DA-CoT) 推理监督四个结构化的中间空间思考，涵盖3D物体定位、抓取可负担性接触几何、成对度量距离和粗略的 SE(3) 路标点，作为训练损失中的显式生成目标。在每个 VLM Transformer 块中的交叉注意力子层提供DA-CoT生成期间对原始256个基元高斯场的直接访问。一个具有混合专家前馈子层的3亿参数流匹配动作专家通过条件 ODE 积分解码7-自由度 delta 动作块，通过双重交叉注意力同时以 VLM 隐状态和 DA-CoT 输出为条件。利用复合损失 $\mathcal{L}_\mathrm{flow} + \mathcal{L}_\mathrm{CoT} + \mathcal{L}_\mathrm{depth}$ 跨三个渐进阶段进行训练，GST-VLA 在 LIBERO 上达到 96.4% (+2.0%)，在 SimplerEnv 上达到 80.2% (+5.4%)。消融实验隔离了每个 GST 组件、每个 DA-CoT 思考和每个训练阶段的贡献，证实了集中在精度要求高的任务上的独立和协同增益。

TLDR: The paper introduces GST-VLA, a Vision-Language-Action model that uses Gaussian Spatial Tokens and Depth-Aware Chain-of-Thought reasoning to improve performance on robotic manipulation tasks, demonstrating significant improvements on the LIBERO and SimplerEnv benchmarks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 GST-VLA，一种视觉-语言-动作模型，它使用高斯空间 Token 和深度感知的思维链推理来提高机器人操作任务的性能，并在 LIBERO 和 SimplerEnv 基准测试中表现出显着改进。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Md Selim Sarowar, Omer Tariq, Sungho Kim

3D UAV Trajectory Estimation and Classification from Internet Videos via Language Model

Abstract (English): Reliable 3D trajectory estimation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) is a fundamental requirement for anti-UAV systems, yet the acquisition of large-scale and accurately annotated trajectory data remains prohibitively expensive. In this work, we present a novel framework that derives UAV 3D trajectories and category information directly from Internet-scale UAV videos, without relying on manual annotations. First, language-driven data acquisition is employed to autonomously discover and collect UAV-related videos, while vision-language reasoning progressively filters task-relevant segments. Second, a training-free cross-modal label generation module is introduced to infer 3D trajectory hypotheses and UAV type cues. Third, a physics-informed refinement process is designed to impose temporal smoothness and kinematic consistency on the estimated trajectories. The resulting video clips and trajectory annotations can be readily utilized for downstream anti-UAV tasks. To assess effectiveness and generalization, we conduct zero-shot transfer experiments on a public, well-annotated 3D UAV benchmark. Results reveal a clear data scaling behavior: as the amount of online video data increases, zero-shot transfer performance on the target dataset improves consistently, without any target-domain training. The proposed method closely approaches the current state-of-the-art, highlighting its robustness and applicability to real-world anti-UAV scenarios. Code and datasets will be released upon acceptance.

摘要（中文）: 无人机(UAV)可靠的3D轨迹估计是反无人机系统的基本要求，然而，大规模且精确标注的轨迹数据的获取成本仍然过高。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，可以直接从互联网规模的无人机视频中推导出无人机3D轨迹和类别信息，而无需依赖人工标注。首先，采用语言驱动的数据采集方法来自动发现和收集与无人机相关的视频，同时视觉-语言推理逐步过滤掉与任务相关的片段。其次，引入了一种无需训练的跨模态标签生成模块，以推断3D轨迹假设和无人机类型线索。第三，设计了一个物理信息引导的细化过程，以对估计的轨迹施加时间平滑性和运动学一致性。由此产生的视频片段和轨迹标注可以很容易地用于下游的反无人机任务。为了评估有效性和泛化性，我们在一个公开且经过良好标注的3D无人机基准数据集上进行了零样本迁移实验。结果显示出清晰的数据规模效应：随着在线视频数据量的增加，目标数据集上的零样本迁移性能持续提高，而无需任何目标域训练。所提出的方法非常接近目前最先进水平，凸显了其在现实世界反无人机场景中的鲁棒性和适用性。代码和数据集将在接受后发布。

TLDR: This paper introduces a novel framework for autonomously deriving 3D UAV trajectories and category information from Internet videos using language models and physics-informed refinement, demonstrating good zero-shot transfer performance on anti-UAV tasks.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种新颖的框架，通过使用语言模型和物理信息约束优化，自主地从互联网视频中提取3D无人机轨迹和类别信息，并在反无人机任务上展示了良好的零样本迁移性能。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Haoxiang Lei, Daotong Wang, Shenghai Yuan, Jianbo Su

ImpedanceDiffusion: Diffusion-Based Global Path Planning for UAV Swarm Navigation with Generative Impedance Control

Abstract (English): Safe swarm navigation in cluttered indoor environment requires long-horizon planning, reactive obstacle avoidance, and adaptive compliance. We propose ImpedanceDiffusion, a hierarchical framework that leverages image-conditioned diffusion-based global path planning with Artificial Potential Field (APF) tracking and semantic-aware variable impedance control for aerial drone swarms. The diffusion model generates geometric global trajectories directly from RGB images without explicit map construction. These trajectories are tracked by an APF-based reactive layer, while a VLM-RAG module performs semantic obstacle classification with 90% retrieval accuracy to adapt impedance parameters for mixed obstacle environments during execution. Two diffusion planners are evaluated: (i) a top-view long-horizon planner using single-pass inference and (ii) a first-person-view (FPV) short-horizon planner deployed via a two-stage inference pipeline. Both planners achieve a 100% trajectory generation rate across twenty static and dynamic experimental configurations and are validated via zero-shot sim-to-real deployment on Crazyflie 2.1 drones through the hierarchical APF-impedance control stack. The top-view planner produces smoother trajectories that yield conservative tracking speeds of 1.0-1.2 m/s near hard obstacles and 0.6-1.0 m/s near soft obstacles. In contrast, the FPV planner generates trajectories with greater local clearance and typically higher speeds, reaching 1.4-2.0 m/s near hard obstacles and up to 1.6 m/s near soft obstacles. Across 20 experimental configurations (100 total runs), the framework achieved a 92% success rate while maintaining stable impedance-based formation control with bounded oscillations and no in-flight collisions, demonstrating reliable and adaptive swarm navigation in cluttered indoor environments.

摘要（中文）: 在杂乱的室内环境中安全地进行无人机集群导航需要长时程规划、反应式避障和自适应顺应性。我们提出了ImpedanceDiffusion，一个分层框架，它利用基于图像条件的扩散全局路径规划，结合人工势场 (APF) 跟踪和语义感知的可变阻抗控制，用于空中无人机群。 扩散模型直接从RGB图像生成几何全局轨迹，无需显式地图构建。这些轨迹由基于APF的反应层跟踪，同时VLM-RAG模块执行语义障碍物分类，检索准确率达到90%，从而在执行过程中自适应混合障碍物环境的阻抗参数。 评估了两种扩散规划器：（i）利用单次推理的俯视长时程规划器和（ii）通过两阶段推理流程部署的第一人称视角 (FPV) 短时程规划器。两种规划器在二十个静态和动态实验配置中都实现了100%的轨迹生成率，并通过分层APF-阻抗控制堆栈在Crazyflie 2.1无人机上进行了零样本的从仿真到真实的部署验证。俯视规划器产生更平滑的轨迹，从而在硬障碍物附近产生1.0-1.2米/秒的保守跟踪速度，在软障碍物附近产生0.6-1.0米/秒的保守跟踪速度。 相比之下，FPV规划器生成的轨迹具有更大的局部间隙和通常更高的速度，在硬障碍物附近达到1.4-2.0米/秒，在软障碍物附近高达1.6米/秒。在20个实验配置（总共100次运行）中，该框架实现了92%的成功率，同时保持了基于阻抗的稳定编队控制，具有有界振荡且无飞行中碰撞，证明了在杂乱室内环境中可靠且自适应的无人机群导航能力。

TLDR: The paper introduces ImpedanceDiffusion, a hierarchical framework using diffusion models for global path planning in UAV swarms, integrating APF tracking and semantic-aware impedance control, demonstrating successful sim-to-real deployment in cluttered environments.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了ImpedanceDiffusion，一个使用扩散模型进行无人机群全局路径规划的层级框架，集成了人工势场跟踪和语义感知的阻抗控制，并在杂乱环境中展示了成功的从仿真到真实的部署。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Faryal Batool, Yasheerah Yaqoot, Muhammad Ahsan Mustafa, Roohan Ahmed Khan, Aleksey Fedoseev, Dzmitry Tsetserukou

FAME: Force-Adaptive RL for Expanding the Manipulation Envelope of a Full-Scale Humanoid

Abstract (English): Maintaining balance under external hand forces is critical for humanoid bimanual manipulation, where interaction forces propagate through the kinematic chain and constrain the feasible manipulation envelope. We propose \textbf{FAME}, a force-adaptive reinforcement learning framework that conditions a standing policy on a learned latent context encoding upper-body joint configuration and bimanual interaction forces. During training, we apply diverse, spherically sampled 3D forces on each hand to inject disturbances in simulation together with an upper-body pose curriculum, exposing the policy to manipulation-induced perturbations across continuously varying arm configurations. At deployment, interaction forces are estimated from the robot dynamics and fed to the same encoder, enabling online adaptation without wrist force/torque sensors. In simulation across five fixed arm configurations with randomized hand forces and commanded base heights, FAME improves mean standing success to 73.84%, compared to 51.40% for the curriculum-only baseline and 29.44% for the base policy. We further deploy the learned policy on a full-scale Unitree H12 humanoid and evaluate robustness in representative load-interaction scenarios, including asymmetric single-arm load and symmetric bimanual load. Code and videos are available on https://fame10.github.io/Fame/

摘要（中文）: 在外部手部作用力下的平衡维持对于人形机器人双手操作至关重要，其中交互力通过运动链传播并约束可行的操作范围。我们提出了一种力自适应强化学习框架 \textbf{FAME}，该框架基于学习到的潜在上下文编码来调节站立策略，该编码包括上半身关节构型和双手交互力。在训练过程中，我们在每个手上施加不同的、球形采样的3D力，以在仿真中注入扰动，并结合上半身姿态课程，使策略暴露于跨连续变化的臂部构型的操作诱导扰动。在部署时，交互力从机器人动力学中估计并馈送到相同的编码器，从而无需腕部力/力矩传感器即可实现在线自适应。在仿真中，针对具有随机手部力和指令基座高度的五个固定臂部构型，FAME 将平均站立成功率提高到 73.84%，而仅使用课程学习的基线为 51.40%，基础策略为 29.44%。我们进一步将学习到的策略部署在全尺寸宇树 H12 人形机器人上，并在具有代表性的负载交互场景中评估其鲁棒性，包括非对称单臂负载和对称双手负载。代码和视频可在 https://fame10.github.io/Fame/ 上获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces FAME, a force-adaptive reinforcement learning framework for humanoid robots to maintain balance under external forces, improving manipulation envelope without relying on force/torque sensors.

简要说明: 本文介绍了一种力自适应强化学习框架FAME，用于帮助人形机器人保持平衡在外部力的作用下， 从而 扩展操作范围，无需依赖力/扭矩传感器。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)

Authors: Niraj Pudasaini, Yutong Zhang, Jensen Lavering, Alessandro Roncone, Nikolaus Correll

Decision-Aware Uncertainty Evaluation of Vision-Language Model-Based Early Action Anticipation for Human-Robot Interaction

Abstract (English): Robots in shared workspaces must interpret human actions from partial, ambiguous observations, where overconfident early predictions can lead to unsafe or disruptive interaction. This challenge is amplified in egocentric views, where viewpoint changes and occlusions increase perceptual noise and ambiguity. As a result, downstream human-robot interaction modules require not only an action hypothesis but also a trustworthy estimate of confidence under partial observation. Recent vision-language model-based approaches have been proposed for short-term action recognition due to their open-vocabulary and context-aware reasoning, but their uncertainty reliability in the temporal-prefix regime is largely uncharacterized. We present the first systematic evaluation of uncertainty in vision-language model-based short-term action recognition for human-robot interaction. We introduce a temporal-prefix evaluation protocol and metrics for calibration and selective prediction. We also characterize miscalibration patterns and failure modes under partial observations. Our study provides the missing reliability evidence needed to use vision-language model predictions in confidence-gated human-robot interaction modules.

摘要（中文）: 共享工作空间中的机器人必须从局部、模糊的观察中解读人类行为，而过于自信的早期预测可能导致不安全或破坏性的交互。在以自我为中心的视角下，这种挑战更加突出，因为视角变化和遮挡会增加感知噪声和模糊性。因此，下游人机交互模块不仅需要一个动作假设，还需要在局部观察下对置信度进行可信赖的估计。最近，基于视觉-语言模型的方法已被提出用于短期动作识别，原因在于它们具有开放词汇和上下文相关推理能力，但它们在时间前缀模式下的不确定性可靠性在很大程度上未被表征。我们提出了第一个针对人机交互中基于视觉-语言模型的短期动作识别的不确定性的系统性评估。我们引入了一种用于校准和选择性预测的时间前缀评估协议和指标。我们还描述了在局部观察下的错误校准模式和失效模式。我们的研究提供了使用置信度门控人机交互模块中所需的、缺失的、视觉-语言模型预测的可靠性证据。

TLDR: This paper systematically evaluates the uncertainty of vision-language models for early action anticipation in human-robot interaction, focusing on temporal prefix scenarios and calibration issues under partial observations.

简要说明: 本文系统评估了视觉-语言模型在人机交互中早期动作预测的不确定性，重点关注时间前缀场景和部分观测下的校准问题。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Zhaoda Du, Michael Bowman, Qiaojie Zheng, Xiaoli Zhang

APPLV: Adaptive Planner Parameter Learning from Vision-Language-Action Model

Abstract (English): Autonomous navigation in highly constrained environments remains challenging for mobile robots. Classical navigation approaches offer safety assurances but require environment-specific parameter tuning; end-to-end learning bypasses parameter tuning but struggles with precise control in constrained spaces. To this end, recent robot learning approaches automate parameter tuning while retaining classical systems' safety, yet still face challenges in generalizing to unseen environments. Recently, Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have shown promise by leveraging foundation models' scene understanding capabilities, but still struggle with precise control and inference latency in navigation tasks. In this paper, we propose Adaptive Planner Parameter Learning from Vision-Language-Action Model (\textsc{applv}). Unlike traditional VLA models that directly output actions, \textsc{applv} leverages pre-trained vision-language models with a regression head to predict planner parameters that configure classical planners. We develop two training strategies: supervised learning fine-tuning from collected navigation trajectories and reinforcement learning fine-tuning to further optimize navigation performance. We evaluate \textsc{applv} across multiple motion planners on the simulated Benchmark Autonomous Robot Navigation (BARN) dataset and in physical robot experiments. Results demonstrate that \textsc{applv} outperforms existing methods in both navigation performance and generalization to unseen environments.

摘要（中文）: 在高度约束环境中进行自主导航对移动机器人来说仍然具有挑战性。经典的导航方法提供安全保障，但需要针对特定环境进行参数调整；端到端学习绕过了参数调整，但在约束空间中难以实现精确控制。为此，近期的机器人学习方法虽然可以在保留经典系统安全性的同时实现参数调整自动化，但在推广到未见环境时仍面临挑战。最近，视觉-语言-动作(VLA)模型通过利用基础模型的场景理解能力展现了潜力，但在导航任务中仍然难以兼顾精确控制和推理延迟。在本文中，我们提出了一种基于视觉-语言-动作模型的自适应规划器参数学习方法(\textsc{applv})。与直接输出动作的传统VLA模型不同，\textsc{applv}利用预训练的视觉-语言模型以及回归头来预测配置经典规划器的规划器参数。我们开发了两种训练策略：从收集到的导航轨迹进行有监督学习微调，以及通过强化学习微调来进一步优化导航性能。我们在模拟的Benchmark Autonomous Robot Navigation (BARN)数据集和物理机器人实验中，评估了\textsc{applv}在多个运动规划器上的表现。结果表明，\textsc{applv}在导航性能和推广到未见环境的能力方面均优于现有方法。

TLDR: The paper introduces APPLV, a method that uses Vision-Language models to predict parameters for classical planners, improving navigation performance and generalization in robotics by combining scene understanding with precise control through supervised and reinforcement learning.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了APPLV，一种利用视觉-语言模型预测经典规划器参数的方法，通过结合场景理解和精确控制，并通过监督学习和强化学习进行训练，提高了机器人在导航方面的性能和泛化能力。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (7/10)

Authors: Yuanjie Lu, Beichen Wang, Zhengqi Wu, Yang Li, Xiaomin Lin, Chengzhi Mao, Xuesu Xiao

HMR-1: Hierarchical Massage Robot with Vision-Language-Model for Embodied Healthcare

Abstract (English): The rapid advancement of Embodied Intelligence has opened transformative opportunities in healthcare, particularly in physical therapy and rehabilitation. However, critical challenges remain in developing robust embodied healthcare solutions, such as the lack of standardized evaluation benchmarks and the scarcity of open-source multimodal acupoint massage datasets. To address these gaps, we construct MedMassage-12K - a multimodal dataset containing 12,190 images with 174,177 QA pairs, covering diverse lighting conditions and backgrounds. Furthermore, we propose a hierarchical embodied massage framework, which includes a high-level acupoint grounding module and a low-level control module. The high-level acupoint grounding module uses multimodal large language models to understand human language and identify acupoint locations, while the low-level control module provides the planned trajectory. Based on this, we evaluate existing MLLMs and establish a benchmark for embodied massage tasks. Additionally, we fine-tune the Qwen-VL model, demonstrating the framework's effectiveness. Physical experiments further confirm the practical applicability of the framework.Our dataset and code are publicly available at https://github.com/Xiaofeng-Han-Res/HMR-1.

摘要（中文）: 具身智能的快速发展为医疗保健领域带来了变革性的机遇，尤其是在物理治疗和康复方面。然而，开发稳健的具身医疗解决方案仍然面临关键挑战，例如缺乏标准化的评估基准和开源多模态穴位按摩数据集的稀缺。为了弥合这些差距，我们构建了MedMassage-12K，一个包含12,190张图像和174,177个问答对的多模态数据集，涵盖了多样化的光照条件和背景。此外，我们提出了一种分层具身按摩框架，包括一个高层穴位定位模块和一个低层控制模块。高层穴位定位模块使用多模态大型语言模型来理解人类语言并识别穴位位置，而低层控制模块提供规划的轨迹。在此基础上，我们评估了现有的MLLM，并为具身按摩任务建立了基准。此外，我们对Qwen-VL模型进行了微调，证明了该框架的有效性。物理实验进一步证实了该框架的实际适用性。我们的数据集和代码可在https://github.com/Xiaofeng-Han-Res/HMR-1公开获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces HMR-1, a hierarchical massage robot framework utilizing vision-language models for acupoint grounding and low-level control, along with the MedMassage-12K dataset for embodied healthcare research.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了HMR-1，一个分层按摩机器人框架，利用视觉-语言模型进行穴位定位和底层控制，以及用于具身医疗保健研究的MedMassage-12K数据集。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Rongtao Xu, Mingming Yu, Xiaofeng Han, Yu Zhang, Kaiyi Hu, Zhe Feng, Zenghuang Fu, Changwei Wang, Weiliang Meng, Xiaopeng Zhang

Scale-Plan: Scalable Language-Enabled Task Planning for Heterogeneous Multi-Robot Teams

Abstract (English): Long-horizon task planning for heterogeneous multi-robot systems is essential for deploying collaborative teams in real-world environments; yet, it remains challenging due to the large volume of perceptual information, much of which is irrelevant to task objectives and burdens planning. Traditional symbolic planners rely on manually constructed problem specifications, limiting scalability and adaptability, while recent large language model (LLM)-based approaches often suffer from hallucinations and weak grounding-i.e., poor alignment between generated plans and actual environmental objects and constraints-in object-rich settings. We present Scale-Plan, a scalable LLM-assisted framework that generates compact, task-relevant problem representations from natural language instructions. Given a PDDL domain specification, Scale-Plan constructs an action graph capturing domain structure and uses shallow LLM reasoning to guide a structured graph search that identifies a minimal subset of relevant actions and objects. By filtering irrelevant information prior to planning, Scale-Plan enables efficient decomposition, allocation, and long-horizon plan generation. We evaluate our approach on complex multi-agent tasks and introduce MAT2-THOR, a cleaned benchmark built on AI2-THOR for reliable evaluation of multi-robot planning systems. Scale-Plan outperforms pure LLM and hybrid LLM-PDDL baselines across all metrics, improving scalability and reliability.

摘要（中文）: 用于异构多机器人系统的长程任务规划对于在真实世界环境中部署协作团队至关重要；然而，由于大量的感知信息（其中大部分与任务目标无关，并加重了规划负担），这仍然具有挑战性。传统的符号规划器依赖于手动构建的问题规范，限制了可扩展性和适应性，而最近基于大型语言模型 (LLM) 的方法经常受到幻觉和弱接地的困扰——即生成的计划与实际环境对象和约束之间的对齐性较差——尤其是在对象丰富的环境中。我们提出了 Scale-Plan，一种可扩展的 LLM 辅助框架，该框架可以从自然语言指令生成紧凑的、与任务相关的的问题表示。给定 PDDL 领域规范，Scale-Plan 构建一个动作图来捕获领域结构，并使用浅层 LLM 推理来引导结构化图搜索，从而识别相关动作和对象的最小子集。通过在规划之前过滤掉无关信息，Scale-Plan 能够实现高效的分解、分配和长程计划生成。我们在复杂的多智能体任务上评估了我们的方法，并引入了 MAT2-THOR，这是一个基于 AI2-THOR 构建的清理过的基准测试，用于可靠地评估多机器人规划系统。Scale-Plan 在所有指标上都优于纯 LLM 和混合 LLM-PDDL 基线，从而提高了可扩展性和可靠性。

TLDR: Scale-Plan is a new LLM-assisted framework for scalable long-horizon task planning for multi-robot systems that generates compact, task-relevant problem representations from natural language instructions, improving performance and scalability compared to previous methods.

简要说明: Scale-Plan是一个新的LLM辅助框架，用于多机器人系统中的可扩展长程任务规划。该框架从自然语言指令生成紧凑的、与任务相关的的问题表示，与以前的方法相比，提高了性能和可扩展性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Piyush Gupta, Sangjae Bae, Jiachen Li, David Isele

CLIPO: Contrastive Learning in Policy Optimization Generalizes RLVR

Abstract (English): Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has significantly advanced the reasoning capacity of Large Language Models (LLMs). However, RLVR solely relies on final answers as outcome rewards, neglecting the correctness of intermediate reasoning steps. Training on these process-wrong but outcome-correct rollouts can lead to hallucination and answer-copying, severely undermining the model's generalization and robustness. To address this, we incorporate a Contrastive Learning mechanism into the Policy Optimization (CLIPO) to generalize the RLVR process. By optimizing a contrastive loss over successful rollouts, CLIPO steers the LLM to capture the invariant structure shared across correct reasoning paths. This provides a more robust cross-trajectory regularization than the original single-path supervision in RLVR, effectively mitigating step-level reasoning inconsistencies and suppressing hallucinatory artifacts. In experiments, CLIPO consistently improves multiple RLVR baselines across diverse reasoning benchmarks, demonstrating uniform improvements in generalization and robustness for policy optimization of LLMs. Our code and training recipes are available at https://github.com/Qwen-Applications/CLIPO.

摘要（中文）: 具有可验证奖励的强化学习(RLVR)显著提升了大型语言模型(LLM)的推理能力。然而，RLVR仅依赖于最终答案作为结果奖励，忽略了中间推理步骤的正确性。在这些过程错误但结果正确的展开上进行训练，会导致幻觉和答案抄袭，严重削弱模型的泛化性和鲁棒性。为了解决这个问题，我们将对比学习机制融入策略优化(CLIPO)中，以推广RLVR过程。通过优化成功展开上的对比损失，CLIPO引导LLM捕捉正确推理路径间共享的不变结构。这提供了比RLVR中原始单路径监督更强的跨轨迹正则化，有效地缓解了步骤级的推理不一致性并抑制了幻觉伪影。在实验中，CLIPO在各种推理基准测试中持续改进多个RLVR基线，证明了LLM策略优化在泛化性和鲁棒性方面的统一提升。我们的代码和训练配方可在https://github.com/Qwen-Applications/CLIPO获得。

TLDR: CLIPO introduces a contrastive learning mechanism to Policy Optimization (PO) within Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) for LLMs, addressing the issue of relying solely on final answer rewards by incorporating correctness of intermediate steps, leading to improved generalization and robustness.

简要说明: CLIPO将对比学习机制引入到LLM的基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）的策略优化（PO）中，通过结合中间步骤的正确性来解决仅依赖最终答案奖励的问题，从而提高泛化性和鲁棒性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Sijia Cui, Pengyu Cheng, Jiajun Song, Yongbo Gai, Guojun Zhang, Zhechao Yu, Jianhe Lin, Xiaoxi Jiang, Guanjun Jiang

Code-Space Response Oracles: Generating Interpretable Multi-Agent Policies with Large Language Models

Abstract (English): Recent advances in multi-agent reinforcement learning, particularly Policy-Space Response Oracles (PSRO), have enabled the computation of approximate game-theoretic equilibria in increasingly complex domains. However, these methods rely on deep reinforcement learning oracles that produce `black-box' neural network policies, making them difficult to interpret, trust or debug. We introduce Code-Space Response Oracles (CSRO), a novel framework that addresses this challenge by replacing RL oracles with Large Language Models (LLMs). CSRO reframes the best response computation as a code generation task, prompting an LLM to generate policies directly as human-readable code. This approach not only yields inherently interpretable policies but also leverages the LLM's pretrained knowledge to discover complex, human-like strategies. We explore multiple ways to construct and enhance an LLM-based oracle: zero-shot prompting, iterative refinement and \emph{AlphaEvolve}, a distributed LLM-based evolutionary system. We demonstrate that CSRO achieves performance competitive with baselines while producing a diverse set of explainable policies. Our work presents a new perspective on multi-agent learning, shifting the focus from optimizing opaque policy parameters to synthesizing interpretable algorithmic behavior.

摘要（中文）: 多智能体强化学习的最新进展，特别是策略空间响应预言机（PSRO），使得在日益复杂的领域中计算近似博弈论均衡成为可能。然而，这些方法依赖于产生“黑盒”神经网络策略的深度强化学习预言机，使得它们难以解释、信任或调试。我们引入了代码空间响应预言机（CSRO），这是一个新颖的框架，通过用大型语言模型（LLMs）替换RL预言机来解决这一挑战。CSRO将最佳响应计算重新定义为代码生成任务，提示LLM直接将策略生成为人类可读的代码。这种方法不仅产生固有的可解释策略，还利用LLM的预训练知识来发现复杂、类人的策略。我们探索了构建和增强基于LLM的预言机的多种方法：零样本提示、迭代细化和\emph{AlphaEvolve}，一个基于分布式LLM的进化系统。我们证明，CSRO在产生多样化的可解释策略的同时，实现了与基线相当的性能。我们的工作提出了多智能体学习的一个新视角，将重点从优化不透明的策略参数转移到合成可解释的算法行为。

TLDR: This paper introduces Code-Space Response Oracles (CSRO), which uses Large Language Models to generate interpretable, code-based policies in multi-agent reinforcement learning, achieving competitive performance while offering explainability.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了代码空间响应预言机(CSRO)，它使用大型语言模型在多智能体强化学习中生成可解释的、基于代码的策略，在实现竞争性性能的同时提供了解释性。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Daniel Hennes, Zun Li, John Schultz, Marc Lanctot

Influencing LLM Multi-Agent Dialogue via Policy-Parameterized Prompts

Abstract (English): Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as a new paradigm for multi-agent systems. However, existing research on the behaviour of LLM-based multi-agents relies on ad hoc prompts and lacks a principled policy perspective. Different from reinforcement learning, we investigate whether prompt-as-action can be parameterized so as to construct a lightweight policy which consists of a sequence of state-action pairs to influence conversational behaviours without training. Our framework regards prompts as actions executed by LLMs, and dynamically constructs prompts through five components based on the current state of the agent. To test the effectiveness of parameterized control, we evaluated the dialogue flow based on five indicators: responsiveness, rebuttal, evidence usage, non-repetition, and stance shift. We conduct experiments using different LLM-driven agents in two discussion scenarios related to the general public and show that prompt parameterization can influence the dialogue dynamics. This result shows that policy-parameterised prompts offer a simple and effective mechanism to influence the dialogue process, which will help the research of multi-agent systems in the direction of social simulation.

摘要（中文）: 大型语言模型（LLMs）已成为多智能体系统的一种新范式。然而，现有关于基于LLM的多智能体行为的研究依赖于临时prompt，缺乏一种基于原则的策略视角。与强化学习不同，我们研究是否可以将“prompt即动作”进行参数化，从而构建一个轻量级策略，该策略由一系列状态-动作对组成，以在无需训练的情况下影响对话行为。我们的框架将prompt视为LLM执行的动作，并基于智能体的当前状态，通过五个组成部分动态构建prompt。为了测试参数化控制的有效性，我们基于五个指标评估了对话流程：响应性，反驳，证据使用，非重复性和立场转变。我们在与公众相关的两个讨论场景中使用不同的LLM驱动的智能体进行了实验，结果表明prompt参数化可以影响对话动态。该结果表明，策略参数化的prompt提供了一种简单有效的机制来影响对话过程，这将有助于多智能体系统在社会模拟方向上的研究。

TLDR: The paper proposes a method to influence LLM multi-agent dialogue by parameterizing prompts as actions, allowing for lightweight policy control of conversational behaviors without training, and demonstrating its effectiveness in two discussion scenarios.

简要说明: 该论文提出了一种通过将提示参数化为动作来影响LLM多智能体对话的方法，从而实现对会话行为的轻量级策略控制，无需训练，并在两种讨论场景中展示了其有效性。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (7/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Hongbo Bo, Jingyu Hu, Weiru Liu

MA-EgoQA: Question Answering over Egocentric Videos from Multiple Embodied Agents

Abstract (English): As embodied models become powerful, humans will collaborate with multiple embodied AI agents at their workplace or home in the future. To ensure better communication between human users and the multi-agent system, it is crucial to interpret incoming information from agents in parallel and refer to the appropriate context for each query. Existing challenges include effectively compressing and communicating high volumes of individual sensory inputs in the form of video and correctly aggregating multiple egocentric videos to construct system-level memory. In this work, we first formally define a novel problem of understanding multiple long-horizon egocentric videos simultaneously collected from embodied agents. To facilitate research in this direction, we introduce MultiAgent-EgoQA (MA-EgoQA), a benchmark designed to systemically evaluate existing models in our scenario. MA-EgoQA provides 1.7k questions unique to multiple egocentric streams, spanning five categories: social interaction, task coordination, theory-of-mind, temporal reasoning, and environmental interaction. We further propose a simple baseline model for MA-EgoQA named EgoMAS, which leverages shared memory across embodied agents and agent-wise dynamic retrieval. Through comprehensive evaluation across diverse baselines and EgoMAS on MA-EgoQA, we find that current approaches are unable to effectively handle multiple egocentric streams, highlighting the need for future advances in system-level understanding across the agents. The code and benchmark are available at https://ma-egoqa.github.io.

摘要（中文）: 随着具身模型变得日益强大，未来人类将在工作场所或家中与多个具身AI代理协作。为了确保人机系统之间更好的沟通，至关重要的是并行地解读来自代理的信息，并针对每个查询参考恰当的上下文。现存的挑战包括有效地压缩和传递大量的个体感官输入（以视频形式呈现），以及正确地聚合多个以自我为中心的视频以构建系统级记忆。在这项工作中，我们首先正式定义了一个新的问题：理解同时从具身代理收集的多个长时程以自我为中心的视频。为了促进这方面的研究，我们引入了MultiAgent-EgoQA (MA-EgoQA) 基准测试，旨在系统性地评估现有模型在我们的场景中的表现。MA-EgoQA提供了 1.7k 个针对多个以自我为中心视频流的独特问题，涵盖五个类别：社交互动、任务协调、心理理论、时间推理和环境互动。我们进一步提出了一个名为EgoMAS的MA-EgoQA简单基线模型，该模型利用了具身代理之间的共享记忆和基于代理的动态检索。通过在MA-EgoQA上对各种基线模型和EgoMAS进行全面评估，我们发现当前的方法无法有效地处理多个以自我为中心的视频流，突出了未来在跨代理系统级理解方面的进展需求。代码和基准测试可在 https://ma-egoqa.github.io 获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces MA-EgoQA, a new benchmark for question answering over multiple egocentric videos collected from embodied agents, highlighting challenges in system-level understanding across agents and proposing a baseline model.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了MA-EgoQA，一个新的基准数据集，用于对从具身智能体收集的多个以自我为中心的视频进行问答，强调了智能体之间的系统级理解面临的挑战，并提出了一个基准模型。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Kangsan Kim, Yanlai Yang, Suji Kim, Woongyeong Yeo, Youngwan Lee, Mengye Ren, Sung Ju Hwang

World2Mind: Cognition Toolkit for Allocentric Spatial Reasoning in Foundation Models

Abstract (English): Achieving robust spatial reasoning remains a fundamental challenge for current Multimodal Foundation Models (MFMs). Existing methods either overfit statistical shortcuts via 3D grounding data or remain confined to 2D visual perception, limiting both spatial reasoning accuracy and generalization in unseen scenarios. Inspired by the spatial cognitive mapping mechanisms of biological intelligence, we propose World2Mind, a training-free spatial intelligence toolkit. At its core, World2Mind leverages 3D reconstruction and instance segmentation models to construct structured spatial cognitive maps, empowering MFMs to proactively acquire targeted spatial knowledge regarding interested landmarks and routes of interest. To provide robust geometric-topological priors, World2Mind synthesizes an Allocentric-Spatial Tree (AST) that uses elliptical parameters to model the top-down layout of landmarks accurately. To mitigate the inherent inaccuracies of 3D reconstruction, we introduce a three-stage reasoning chain comprising tool invocation assessment, modality-decoupled cue collection, and geometry-semantics interwoven reasoning. Extensive experiments demonstrate that World2Mind boosts the performance of frontier models, such as GPT-5.2, by 5%~18%. Astonishingly, relying solely on the AST-structured text, purely text-only foundation models can perform complex 3D spatial reasoning, achieving performance approaching that of advanced multimodal models.

摘要（中文）: 实现稳健的空间推理仍然是当前多模态基础模型 (MFMs) 的一个根本挑战。现有方法要么通过3D接地数据过度拟合统计捷径，要么仍然局限于2D视觉感知，从而限制了空间推理的准确性和在未见场景中的泛化能力。受生物智能空间认知地图机制的启发，我们提出了World2Mind，一种无需训练的空间智能工具包。其核心在于，World2Mind利用3D重建和实例分割模型构建结构化空间认知地图，使MFMs能够主动获取关于感兴趣地标和路线的定向空间知识。为了提供稳健的几何-拓扑先验，World2Mind合成了一个以自我中心的空间树 (AST)，该树使用椭圆参数来精确地建模地标的自顶向下布局。为了缓解3D重建的固有不准确性，我们引入了一个三阶段推理链，包括工具调用评估、模态解耦的线索收集以及几何-语义交织的推理过程。大量的实验表明，World2Mind将GPT-5.2等前沿模型的性能提升了5%~18%。令人惊讶的是，仅依靠AST结构化的文本，纯文本基础模型就可以执行复杂的3D空间推理，其性能接近于先进的多模态模型。

TLDR: The paper introduces World2Mind, a training-free spatial intelligence toolkit that enhances spatial reasoning capabilities in foundation models by constructing structured spatial cognitive maps and leveraging an Allocentric-Spatial Tree (AST). It significantly improves performance on spatial reasoning tasks, even enabling text-only models to perform complex 3D reasoning.

简要说明: 该论文介绍World2Mind，一个免训练的空间智能工具包，通过构建结构化的空间认知地图并利用以自我为中心的空间树（AST），增强了基础模型中的空间推理能力。它显著提高了空间推理任务的性能，甚至使纯文本模型能够执行复杂的3D推理。

Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Shouwei Ruan, Bin Wang, Zhenyu Wu, Qihui Zhu, Yuxiang Zhang, Hang Su, Yubin Wang

EXPLORE-Bench: Egocentric Scene Prediction with Long-Horizon Reasoning

Abstract (English): Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly considered as a foundation for embodied agents, yet it remains unclear whether they can reliably reason about the long-term physical consequences of actions from an egocentric viewpoint. We study this gap through a new task, Egocentric Scene Prediction with LOng-horizon REasoning: given an initial-scene image and a sequence of atomic action descriptions, a model is asked to predict the final scene after all actions are executed. To enable systematic evaluation, we introduce EXPLORE-Bench, a benchmark curated from real first-person videos spanning diverse scenarios. Each instance pairs long action sequences with structured final-scene annotations, including object categories, visual attributes, and inter-object relations, which supports fine-grained, quantitative assessment. Experiments on a range of proprietary and open-source MLLMs reveal a significant performance gap to humans, indicating that long-horizon egocentric reasoning remains a major challenge. We further analyze test-time scaling via stepwise reasoning and show that decomposing long action sequences can improve performance to some extent, while incurring non-trivial computational overhead. Overall, EXPLORE-Bench provides a principled testbed for measuring and advancing long-horizon reasoning for egocentric embodied perception.

摘要（中文）: 多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）正日益被视为具身智能体的基础，但它们是否能够可靠地从自我中心视角推断动作的长期物理后果仍不清楚。我们通过一项新任务来研究这一差距，该任务名为“基于长程推理的自我中心场景预测”：给定一个初始场景图像和一系列原子动作描述，模型需要预测执行所有动作后的最终场景。为了实现系统性评估，我们引入了EXPLORE-Bench，这是一个从涵盖各种场景的真实第一人称视频中整理的基准。每个实例都将长动作序列与结构化的最终场景标注配对，包括对象类别、视觉属性和对象间关系，这支持了细粒度的定量评估。在各种专有和开源MLLM上的实验表明，与人类相比存在显著的性能差距，表明长程自我中心推理仍然是一个主要挑战。我们进一步分析了通过逐步推理实现的测试时缩放，并表明分解长动作序列可以在一定程度上提高性能，同时产生不可忽略的计算开销。总而言之，EXPLORE-Bench为测量和推进用于自我中心具身感知的长程推理提供了一个有原则的测试平台。

TLDR: The paper introduces EXPLORE-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating the long-horizon reasoning capabilities of multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in egocentric scene prediction tasks, revealing a significant performance gap compared to humans.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 EXPLORE-Bench，一个用于评估多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）在以自我为中心的场景预测任务中长期推理能力的基准，揭示了与人类相比的显著性能差距。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Chengjun Yu, Xuhan Zhu, Chaoqun Du, Pengfei Yu, Wei Zhai, Yang Cao, Zheng-Jun Zha

AutoAgent: Evolving Cognition and Elastic Memory Orchestration for Adaptive Agents

Abstract (English): Autonomous agent frameworks still struggle to reconcile long-term experiential learning with real-time, context-sensitive decision-making. In practice, this gap appears as static cognition, rigid workflow dependence, and inefficient context usage, which jointly limit adaptability in open-ended and non-stationary environments. To address these limitations, we present AutoAgent, a self-evolving multi-agent framework built on three tightly coupled components: evolving cognition, on-the-fly contextual decision-making, and elastic memory orchestration. At the core of AutoAgent, each agent maintains structured prompt-level cognition over tools, self-capabilities, peer expertise, and task knowledge. During execution, this cognition is combined with live task context to select actions from a unified space that includes tool calls, LLM-based generation, and inter-agent requests. To support efficient long-horizon reasoning, an Elastic Memory Orchestrator dynamically organizes interaction history by preserving raw records, compressing redundant trajectories, and constructing reusable episodic abstractions, thereby reducing token overhead while retaining decision-critical evidence. These components are integrated through a closed-loop cognitive evolution process that aligns intended actions with observed outcomes to continuously update cognition and expand reusable skills, without external retraining. Empirical results across retrieval-augmented reasoning, tool-augmented agent benchmarks, and embodied task environments show that AutoAgent consistently improves task success, tool-use efficiency, and collaborative robustness over static and memory-augmented baselines. Overall, AutoAgent provides a unified and practical foundation for adaptive autonomous agents that must learn from experience while making reliable context-aware decisions in dynamic environments.

摘要（中文）: 自主Agent框架仍然难以协调长期经验学习与实时、上下文敏感的决策。 在实践中，这种差距表现为静态认知、僵化的工作流依赖以及低效的上下文利用，这些共同限制了在开放式和非平稳环境中的适应性。 为了解决这些限制，我们提出了AutoAgent，一个建立在三个紧密耦合组件之上的自进化多Agent框架：进化认知、即时上下文决策以及弹性记忆编排。 在AutoAgent的核心，每个Agent都维护着关于工具、自身能力、同伴专业知识和任务知识的结构化提示级别认知。 在执行过程中，这种认知与实时任务上下文相结合，以从统一的空间中选择动作，该空间包括工具调用、基于LLM的生成以及Agent间请求。 为了支持高效的长期推理，弹性记忆编排器动态地组织交互历史，通过保存原始记录、压缩冗余轨迹以及构建可重用的情景抽象，从而减少token开销，同时保留决策性的关键证据。 这些组件通过一个闭环认知进化过程集成，该过程将预期动作与观察到的结果对齐，以持续更新认知并扩展可重用的技能，而无需外部重新训练。 在检索增强推理、工具增强Agent基准测试和具身任务环境中的实证结果表明，相对于静态和记忆增强基线，AutoAgent始终提高任务成功率、工具使用效率和协作鲁棒性。 总而言之，AutoAgent为自适应自主Agent提供了一个统一且实用的基础，这些Agent必须从经验中学习，同时在动态环境中做出可靠的上下文感知决策。

TLDR: AutoAgent is a self-evolving multi-agent framework with evolving cognition, on-the-fly contextual decision-making, and elastic memory orchestration, demonstrating improved task success and robustness in dynamic environments.

简要说明: AutoAgent是一个自进化的多智能体框架，具有不断演进的认知、即时情境决策和弹性记忆编排，在动态环境中展示了任务成功率和鲁棒性的提高。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Xiaoxing Wang, Ning Liao, Shikun Wei, Chen Tang, Feiyu Xiong

PRECEPT: Planning Resilience via Experience, Context Engineering & Probing Trajectories A Unified Framework for Test-Time Adaptation with Compositional Rule Learning and Pareto-Guided Prompt Evolution

Abstract (English): LLM agents that store knowledge as natural language suffer steep retrieval degradation as condition count grows, often struggle to compose learned rules reliably, and typically lack explicit mechanisms to detect stale or adversarial knowledge. We introduce PRECEPT, a unified framework for test-time adaptation with three tightly coupled components: (1) deterministic exact-match rule retrieval over structured condition keys, (2) conflict-aware memory with Bayesian source reliability and threshold-based rule invalidation, and (3) COMPASS, a Pareto-guided prompt-evolution outer loop. Exact retrieval eliminates partial-match interpretation errors on the deterministic path (0% by construction, vs 94.4% under Theorem~B.6's independence model at N=10) and supports compositional stacking through a semantic tier hierarchy; conflict-aware memory resolves static--dynamic disagreements and supports drift adaptation; COMPASS evaluates prompts through the same end-to-end execution pipeline. Results (9--10 seeds): PRECEPT achieves a +41.1pp first-try advantage over Full Reflexion (d>1.9), +33.3pp compositional generalization (d=1.55), 100% $P_1$ on 2-way logistics compositions (d=2.64), +40--55pp continuous learning gains, strong eventual robustness under adversarial static knowledge (100% logistics with adversarial SK active; partial recovery on integration), +55.0pp drift recovery (d=0.95, p=0.031), and 61% fewer steps. Core comparisons are statistically significant, often at p<0.001.

摘要（中文）: 将知识存储为自然语言的LLM智能体，会随着条件数量的增加遭受急剧的检索性能下降，经常难以可靠地组合已学习的规则，并且通常缺乏显式机制来检测过时或对抗性知识。我们引入了PRECEPT，这是一个用于测试时自适应的统一框架，包含三个紧密耦合的组件：（1）基于结构化条件键的确定性精确匹配规则检索；（2）具有贝叶斯源可靠性和基于阈值的规则失效的冲突感知记忆；以及（3）COMPASS，一个帕累托引导的提示进化外循环。精确检索消除了确定性路径上的部分匹配解释错误（根据构造为0%，而根据定理B.6在N=10时的独立模型下为94.4%），并且支持通过语义层级结构实现的组合堆叠；冲突感知记忆解决了静态-动态不一致问题，并支持漂移适应；COMPASS通过相同的端到端执行流程来评估提示。 结果（9-10个种子）：相对于完整反思（Full Reflexion）(d>1.9)，PRECEPT在首次尝试时获得了+41.1个百分点的优势，+33.3个百分点的组合泛化(d=1.55)，在双向逻辑组合上达到100%的$P_1$ (d=2.64)，+40--55个百分点的持续学习增益，在对抗性静态知识下表现出强大的最终鲁棒性（在对抗性静态知识激活时，逻辑组合达到100%；集成时部分恢复），+55.0个百分点的漂移恢复(d=0.95, p=0.031)以及步骤减少61%。核心比较在统计学上具有显著性，通常在p<0.001时。

TLDR: The paper introduces PRECEPT, a framework for test-time adaptation of LLM agents using deterministic rule retrieval, conflict-aware memory, and Pareto-guided prompt evolution, showing significant improvements in various tasks involving rule composition and knowledge adaptation.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了PRECEPT，一种用于LLM agent在测试时自适应的框架，它使用确定性规则检索、冲突感知记忆和Pareto引导的提示进化，在涉及规则组合和知识适应的各种任务中显示出显著的改进。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (9/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Arash Shahmansoori

Beyond Scaling: Assessing Strategic Reasoning and Rapid Decision-Making Capability of LLMs in Zero-sum Environments

Abstract (English): Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved strong performance on static reasoning benchmarks, yet their effectiveness as interactive agents operating in adversarial, time-sensitive environments remains poorly understood. Existing evaluations largely treat reasoning as a single-shot capability, overlooking the challenges of opponent-aware decision-making, temporal constraints, and execution under pressure. This paper introduces Strategic Tactical Agent Reasoning (STAR) Benchmark, a multi-agent evaluation framework that assesses LLMs through 1v1 zero-sum competitive interactions, framing reasoning as an iterative, adaptive decision-making process. STAR supports both turn-based and real-time settings, enabling controlled analysis of long-horizon strategic planning and fast-paced tactical execution within a unified environment. Built on a modular architecture with a standardized API and fully implemented execution engine, STAR facilitates reproducible evaluation and flexible task customization. To move beyond binary win-loss outcomes, we introduce a Strategic Evaluation Suite that assesses not only competitive success but also the quality of strategic behavior, such as execution efficiency and outcome stability. Extensive pairwise evaluations reveal a pronounced strategy-execution gap: while reasoning-intensive models dominate turn-based settings, their inference latency often leads to inferior performance in real-time scenarios, where faster instruction-tuned models prevail. These results show that strategic intelligence in interactive environments depends not only on reasoning depth, but also on the ability to translate plans into timely actions, positioning STAR as a principled benchmark for studying this trade-off in competitive, dynamic settings.

摘要（中文）: 大型语言模型（LLMs）在静态推理基准测试中取得了优异的性能，但它们作为在对抗性、时间敏感环境中运行的交互式代理的有效性仍然知之甚少。现有的评估主要将推理视为一种单次能力，忽略了对手感知决策制定、时间约束以及压力下执行的挑战。本文介绍了一种名为战略战术代理推理（STAR）基准测试的多代理评估框架，该框架通过1v1零和竞争性互动来评估 LLMs，并将推理构建为一个迭代的、自适应的决策过程。STAR 支持回合制和实时设置，从而能够在统一的环境中对长期战略规划和快节奏战术执行进行受控分析。STAR 基于模块化架构，具有标准化的 API 和完全实现的执行引擎，可促进可重复的评估和灵活的任务定制。为了超越二元输赢结果，我们引入了一个战略评估套件，该套件不仅评估竞争成功，还评估战略行为的质量，例如执行效率和结果稳定性。广泛的成对评估揭示了一个明显的战略-执行差距：虽然推理密集型模型在回合制设置中占据主导地位，但它们的推理延迟通常会导致在实时场景中表现较差，而速度更快的指令调整模型则占据优势。这些结果表明，交互式环境中的战略智能不仅取决于推理深度，还取决于将计划转化为及时行动的能力，从而使 STAR 成为研究竞争性、动态环境中这种权衡的原则性基准。

TLDR: This paper introduces the STAR benchmark for evaluating LLMs in zero-sum, competitive environments, highlighting a strategy-execution trade-off between reasoning depth and real-time performance.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了STAR基准，用于评估LLM在零和竞争环境中的性能，强调了推理深度和实时性能之间的策略执行权衡。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yang Li, Xing Chen, Yutao Liu, Gege Qi, Yanxian BI, Zizhe Wang, Yunjian Zhang, Yao Zhu

Real-Time Trust Verification for Safe Agentic Actions using TrustBench

Abstract (English): As large language models evolve from conversational assistants to autonomous agents, ensuring trustworthiness requires a fundamental shift from post-hoc evaluation to real-time action verification. Current frameworks like AgentBench evaluate task completion, while TrustLLM and HELM assess output quality after generation. However, none of these prevent harmful actions during agent execution. We present TrustBench, a dual-mode framework that (1) benchmarks trust across multiple dimensions using both traditional metrics and LLM-as-a-Judge evaluations, and (2) provides a toolkit agents invoke before taking actions to verify safety and reliability. Unlike existing approaches, TrustBench intervenes at the critical decision point: after an agent formulates an action but before execution. Domain-specific plugins encode specialized safety requirements for healthcare, finance, and technical domains. Across multiple agentic tasks, TrustBench reduced harmful actions by 87%. Domain-specific plugins outperformed generic verification, achieving 35% greater harm reduction. With sub-200ms latency, TrustBench enables practical real-time trust verification for autonomous agents.

摘要（中文）: 随着大型语言模型从对话助手演变为自主智能体，确保其可信度需要从事后评估转变为实时行动验证的根本性转变。当前的框架，如AgentBench，评估任务完成情况，而TrustLLM和HELM则评估生成后的输出质量。然而，这些框架都不能阻止智能体执行过程中的有害行为。我们提出了TrustBench，一个双模式框架，它（1）使用传统指标和LLM-as-a-Judge评估，对多个维度上的信任进行基准测试；（2）提供一个工具包，智能体在采取行动前调用它来验证安全性和可靠性。与现有方法不同，TrustBench在关键决策点进行干预：即智能体形成行动之后，但在执行之前。特定领域的插件编码了针对医疗、金融和技术领域的专门安全需求。在多个智能体任务中，TrustBench减少了87%的有害行为。特定领域插件的性能优于通用验证，实现了35%更高的危害减少率。TrustBench具有低于200毫秒的延迟，能够对自主智能体进行实用的实时信任验证。

TLDR: TrustBench is introduced as a dual-mode framework for real-time trust verification in LLM agents, intervening before action execution to reduce harmful actions by leveraging domain-specific plugins.

简要说明: TrustBench 是一种双模式框架，用于 LLM 代理中的实时信任验证，通过利用特定领域的插件在执行操作前进行干预，以减少有害操作。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (9/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Tavishi Sharma, Vinayak Sharma, Pragya Sharma

Meissa: Multi-modal Medical Agentic Intelligence

Abstract (English): Multi-modal large language models (MM-LLMs) have shown strong performance in medical image understanding and clinical reasoning. Recent medical agent systems extend them with tool use and multi-agent collaboration, enabling complex decision-making. However, these systems rely almost entirely on frontier models (e.g., GPT), whose API-based deployment incurs high cost, high latency, and privacy risks that conflict with on-premise clinical requirements. We present Meissa, a lightweight 4B-parameter medical MM-LLM that brings agentic capability offline. Instead of imitating static answers, Meissa learns both when to engage external interaction (strategy selection) and how to execute multi-step interaction (strategy execution) by distilling structured trajectories from frontier models. Specifically, we propose: (1) Unified trajectory modeling: trajectories (reasoning and action traces) are represented within a single state-action-observation formalism, allowing one model to generalize across heterogeneous medical environments. (2) Three-tier stratified supervision: the model's own errors trigger progressive escalation from direct reasoning to tool-augmented and multi-agent interaction, explicitly learning difficulty-aware strategy selection. (3) Prospective-retrospective supervision: pairing exploratory forward traces with hindsight-rationalized execution traces enables stable learning of effective interaction policies. Trained on 40K curated trajectories, Meissa matches or exceeds proprietary frontier agents in 10 of 16 evaluation settings across 13 medical benchmarks spanning radiology, pathology, and clinical reasoning. Using over 25x fewer parameters than typical frontier models like Gemini-3, Meissa operates fully offline with 22x lower end-to-end latency compared to API-based deployment. Data, models, and environments are released at https://github.com/Schuture/Meissa.

摘要（中文）: 多模态大型语言模型（MM-LLMs）在医学图像理解和临床推理方面表现出强大的性能。 近期的医学代理系统通过工具使用和多智能体协作对其进行扩展，从而实现复杂的决策制定。 然而，这些系统几乎完全依赖于前沿模型（例如，GPT），其基于API的部署会产生高成本、高延迟和隐私风险，这与本地部署的临床需求相冲突。 我们提出了Meissa，一个轻量级的40亿参数医学MM-LLM，可将代理能力带到离线状态。 Meissa 没有模仿静态答案，而是通过从前沿模型中提取结构化轨迹来学习何时进行外部交互（策略选择）以及如何执行多步骤交互（策略执行）。 具体而言，我们提出：（1）统一的轨迹建模：轨迹（推理和行动轨迹）在单一的状态-行动-观察形式主义中表示，从而允许一个模型在异构医学环境中进行泛化。 （2）三层分层监督：模型自身的错误会触发从直接推理到工具增强和多智能体交互的渐进式升级，从而显式地学习具有难度意识的策略选择。 （3）前瞻-回顾式监督：将探索性的向前轨迹与事后合理化的执行轨迹配对，能够稳定地学习有效的交互策略。 Meissa在4万个精心设计的轨迹上进行训练，在涵盖放射学、病理学和临床推理的13个医学基准测试的16个评估设置中的10个中，匹配或超过了专有的前沿代理。 Meissa使用的参数比典型的 Gemini-3 等前沿模型少25倍以上，可以完全离线运行，端到端延迟比基于API的部署低22倍。 数据、模型和环境已在 https://github.com/Schuture/Meissa 上发布。

TLDR: The paper introduces Meissa, a lightweight medical MM-LLM with agentic capabilities that can operate offline by distilling structured trajectories from frontier models, achieving comparable or better performance with significantly lower latency and cost.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 Meissa，一个轻量级的医疗多模态LLM，具有agent功能，可以通过提取前沿模型的结构化轨迹进行离线操作，以显着降低的延迟和成本实现可比较的甚至更好的性能。

Authors: Yixiong Chen, Xinyi Bai, Yue Pan, Zongwei Zhou, Alan Yuille

Using Vision Language Foundation Models to Generate Plant Simulation Configurations via In-Context Learning

Abstract (English): This paper introduces a synthetic benchmark to evaluate the performance of vision language models (VLMs) in generating plant simulation configurations for digital twins. While functional-structural plant models (FSPMs) are useful tools for simulating biophysical processes in agricultural environments, their high complexity and low throughput create bottlenecks for deployment at scale. We propose a novel approach that leverages state-of-the-art open-source VLMs -- Gemma 3 and Qwen3-VL -- to directly generate simulation parameters in JSON format from drone-based remote sensing images. Using a synthetic cowpea plot dataset generated via the Helios 3D procedural plant generation library, we tested five in-context learning methods and evaluated the models across three categories: JSON integrity, geometric evaluations, and biophysical evaluations. Our results show that while VLMs can interpret structural metadata and estimate parameters like plant count and sun azimuth, they often exhibit performance degradation due to contextual bias or rely on dataset means when visual cues are insufficient. Validation on a real-world drone orthophoto dataset and an ablation study using a blind baseline further characterize the models' reasoning capabilities versus their reliance on contextual priors. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first study to utilize VLMs to generate structural JSON configurations for plant simulations, providing a scalable framework for reconstruction 3D plots for digital twin in agriculture.

摘要（中文）: 本文介绍了一个合成基准，用于评估视觉语言模型（VLMs）在生成植物仿真配置，用于构建数字孪生方面的性能。虽然功能-结构植物模型（FSPMs）是模拟农业环境中生物物理过程的有用工具，但其高复杂性和低吞吐量阻碍了大规模部署。我们提出了一种新方法，利用最先进的开源VLMs——Gemma 3和Qwen3-VL——直接从基于无人机的遥感图像生成JSON格式的仿真参数。 使用通过Helios 3D程序化植物生成库生成的合成豇豆地块数据集，我们测试了五种上下文学习方法，并在三个类别中评估了模型：JSON完整性、几何评估和生物物理评估。我们的结果表明，虽然VLMs可以解释结构元数据并估计诸如植物计数和太阳方位角之类的参数，但由于上下文偏差，它们经常表现出性能下降，或者在视觉线索不足时依赖于数据集均值。 在真实世界的无人机正射影像数据集上的验证以及使用盲基线的消融研究进一步表征了模型的推理能力与它们对上下文先验的依赖性。 据我们所知，这是第一项利用VLMs生成植物模拟结构JSON配置的研究，为农业数字孪生中重建3D地块提供了一个可扩展的框架。

TLDR: This paper explores using Vision Language Models (VLMs) for generating plant simulation configurations from drone imagery, evaluating their performance on a synthetic benchmark and a real-world dataset, showing promise and limitations.

简要说明: 本文探索了使用视觉语言模型（VLMs）从无人机图像生成植物模拟配置，并在合成基准和真实世界数据集上评估了它们的性能，展示了前景和局限性。

Authors: Heesup Yun, Isaac Kazuo Uyehara, Earl Ranario, Lars Lundqvist, Christine H. Diepenbrock, Brian N. Bailey, J. Mason Earles

Tool Receipts, Not Zero-Knowledge Proofs: Practical Hallucination Detection for AI Agents

Abstract (English): AI agents that execute tasks via tool calls frequently hallucinate results - fabricating tool executions, misstating output counts, or presenting inferences as facts. Recent approaches to verifiable AI inference rely on zero-knowledge proofs, which provide cryptographic guarantees but impose minutes of proving time per query, making them impractical for interactive agents. We propose NabaOS, a lightweight verification framework inspired by Indian epistemology (Nyaya Shastra), which classifies every claim in an LLM response by its epistemic source (pramana): direct tool output (pratyaksha), inference (anumana), external testimony (shabda), absence (abhava), or ungrounded opinion. Our runtime generates HMAC-signed tool execution receipts that the LLM cannot forge, then cross-references claims against these receipts to detect hallucinations in real time. We evaluate on NyayaVerifyBench, a new benchmark of 1,800 agent response scenarios across four languages with injected hallucinations of six types. NabaOS detects 94.2% of fabricated tool references, 87.6% of count misstatements, and 91.3% of false absence claims, with <15ms verification overhead per response. For deep delegation (agents performing multi-step web tasks), our cross-checking protocol catches 78.4% of URL fabrications via independent re-fetching. We compare against five approaches: zkLLM (cryptographic proofs, 180s/query), TOPLOC (locality-sensitive hashing), SPEX (sampling-based proof of execution), tensor commitments, and self-consistency checking. NabaOS achieves the best cost-latency-coverage trade-off for interactive agents: 94.2% coverage at <15ms versus zkLLM's near-perfect coverage at 180,000ms. For interactive agents, practical receipt-based verification provides better cost-benefit than cryptographic proofs, and epistemic classification gives users actionable trust signals rather than binary judgments.

摘要（中文）: 以下是翻译结果： 通过工具调用执行任务的AI代理经常出现结果幻觉——捏造工具执行、错误陈述输出数量或将推断呈现为事实。最近用于可验证AI推理的方法依赖于零知识证明，它提供密码学保证，但每个查询需要数分钟的证明时间，这使得它们对交互式代理来说不切实际。我们提出了NabaOS，一个受印度知识论（正理派）启发的轻量级验证框架，它根据认识论来源（量）对LLM响应中的每个声明进行分类：直接工具输出（现量）、推断（比量）、外部证词（声量）、不存在（非量）或无根据的观点。我们的运行时生成LLM无法伪造的HMAC签名的工具执行收据，然后将声明与这些收据进行交叉引用，以实时检测幻觉。我们在NyayaVerifyBench上进行了评估，这是一个新的基准测试，包含跨四种语言的1,800个代理响应场景，并注入了六种类型的幻觉。NabaOS检测到94.2%的捏造工具引用、87.6%的数量错误陈述和91.3%的虚假不存在声明，每个响应的验证开销<15ms。对于深度委托（代理执行多步骤网络任务），我们的交叉检查协议通过独立重新获取捕获了78.4%的URL捏造。我们与五种方法进行了比较：zkLLM（密码学证明，180秒/查询）、TOPLOC（局部敏感哈希）、SPEX（基于采样的执行证明）、张量承诺和自洽性检查。NabaOS实现了交互式代理的最佳成本-延迟-覆盖率权衡：<15ms时达到94.2%的覆盖率，而zkLLM在180,000ms时达到接近完美的覆盖率。对于交互式代理，基于收据的实用验证比密码学证明提供更好的性价比，并且认识论分类为用户提供了可操作的信任信号，而不是二元判断。

TLDR: This paper presents NabaOS, a practical and efficient framework for detecting hallucinations in AI agents by using HMAC-signed tool execution receipts and epistemic classification, achieving high accuracy with low latency compared to zero-knowledge proofs.

简要说明: 本文介绍NabaOS，一个实用且高效的框架，通过使用HMAC签名的工具执行收据和认知分类来检测AI代理中的幻觉，与零知识证明相比，实现了高准确度和低延迟。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)

Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Abhinaba Basu

Quantifying the Accuracy and Cost Impact of Design Decisions in Budget-Constrained Agentic LLM Search

Abstract (English): Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems combine iterative search, planning prompts, and retrieval backends, but deployed settings impose explicit budgets on tool calls and completion tokens. We present a controlled measurement study of how search depth, retrieval strategy, and completion budget affect accuracy and cost under fixed constraints. Using Budget-Constrained Agentic Search (BCAS), a model-agnostic evaluation harness that surfaces remaining budget and gates tool use, we run comparisons across six LLMs and three question-answering benchmarks. Across models and datasets, accuracy improves with additional searches up to a small cap, hybrid lexical and dense retrieval with lightweight re-ranking produces the largest average gains in our ablation grid, and larger completion budgets are most helpful on HotpotQA-style synthesis. These results provide practical guidance for configuring budgeted agentic retrieval pipelines and are accompanied by reproducible prompts and evaluation settings.

摘要（中文）: 主动增强生成 (RAG) 系统结合了迭代搜索、规划提示和检索后端，但已部署的设置对工具调用和补全令牌施加了明确的预算约束。我们提出了一项受控测量研究，旨在探讨在固定约束下，搜索深度、检索策略和补全预算如何影响准确性和成本。借助预算约束的主动搜索 (BCAS)，这是一种模型无关的评估工具，可显示剩余预算并控制工具使用，我们对六个大型语言模型和三个问答基准进行了比较。在不同的模型和数据集上，准确率会随着搜索次数的增加而提高，直至达到一个较小的上限；在我们的消融网格中，带有轻量级重新排序的混合词法和密集检索产生了最大的平均收益；而更大的补全预算对 HotpotQA 风格的合成最有帮助。这些结果为配置有预算限制的主动检索管道提供了实践指导，并附带可重现的提示和评估设置。

TLDR: This paper presents a budget-constrained evaluation of agentic RAG systems, analyzing how search depth, retrieval strategy, and completion budget impact accuracy and cost across various LLMs and QA benchmarks, offering practical guidance for configuration.

简要说明: 本文提出了一种预算约束下的Agentic RAG系统评估方法，分析了搜索深度、检索策略和补全预算如何影响不同LLM和QA基准测试的准确性和成本，并为配置提供实用指导。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)

Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Kyle McCleary, James Ghawaly

MASEval: Extending Multi-Agent Evaluation from Models to Systems

Abstract (English): The rapid adoption of LLM-based agentic systems has produced a rich ecosystem of frameworks (smolagents, LangGraph, AutoGen, CAMEL, LlamaIndex, i.a.). Yet existing benchmarks are model-centric: they fix the agentic setup and do not compare other system components. We argue that implementation decisions substantially impact performance, including choices such as topology, orchestration logic, and error handling. MASEval addresses this evaluation gap with a framework-agnostic library that treats the entire system as the unit of analysis. Through a systematic system-level comparison across 3 benchmarks, 3 models, and 3 frameworks, we find that framework choice matters as much as model choice. MASEval allows researchers to explore all components of agentic systems, opening new avenues for principled system design, and practitioners to identify the best implementation for their use case. MASEval is available under the MIT licence https://github.com/parameterlab/MASEval.

摘要（中文）: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体系统正被迅速采用，由此产生了丰富的框架生态（例如，smolagents、LangGraph、AutoGen、CAMEL、LlamaIndex）。然而，现有的基准测试都是以模型为中心的：它们固定智能体设置，而不比较其他系统组件。我们认为，实现决策对性能有显著影响，包括拓扑结构、编排逻辑和错误处理等选择。MASEval 通过一个与框架无关的库来弥补这一评估差距，并将整个系统视为分析单元。通过在 3 个基准测试、3 个模型和 3 个框架之间进行系统的系统级比较，我们发现框架选择与模型选择同等重要。MASEval 允许研究人员探索智能体系统的所有组件，为有原则的系统设计开辟新途径，并帮助从业者为他们的用例确定最佳实现方案。 MASEval 在 MIT 许可下可用，地址为：https://github.com/parameterlab/MASEval。

TLDR: MASEval is a new framework for evaluating multi-agent systems as a whole, considering system-level implementation details, and the authors find that framework choice is as important as model choice. This framework addresses the evaluation gap in the agentic system, allowing researchers and practitioners to explore all the components of agentic systems to improve design and implementation.

简要说明: MASEval是一个新的框架，用于评估整个多智能体系统，考虑到系统层面的实现细节。作者发现框架的选择与模型的选择同样重要。这个框架解决了智能体系统中的评估差距，允许研究人员和实践者探索智能体系统的所有组件，以改进设计和实现。

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Cornelius Emde, Alexander Rubinstein, Anmol Goel, Ahmed Heakl, Sangdoo Yun, Seong Joon Oh, Martin Gubri

Test-Driven AI Agent Definition (TDAD): Compiling Tool-Using Agents from Behavioral Specifications

Abstract (English): We present Test-Driven AI Agent Definition (TDAD), a methodology that treats agent prompts as compiled artifacts: engineers provide behavioral specifications, a coding agent converts them into executable tests, and a second coding agent iteratively refines the prompt until tests pass. Deploying tool-using LLM agents in production requires measurable behavioral compliance that current development practices cannot provide. Small prompt changes cause silent regressions, tool misuse goes undetected, and policy violations emerge only after deployment. To mitigate specification gaming, TDAD introduces three mechanisms: (1) visible/hidden test splits that withhold evaluation tests during compilation, (2) semantic mutation testing via a post-compilation agent that generates plausible faulty prompt variants, with the harness measuring whether the test suite detects them, and (3) spec evolution scenarios that quantify regression safety when requirements change. We evaluate TDAD on SpecSuite-Core, a benchmark of four deeply-specified agents spanning policy compliance, grounded analytics, runbook adherence, and deterministic enforcement. Across 24 independent trials, TDAD achieves 92% v1 compilation success with 97% mean hidden pass rate; evolved specifications compile at 58%, with most failed runs passing all visible tests except 1-2, and show 86-100% mutation scores, 78% v2 hidden pass rate, and 97% regression safety scores. The implementation is available as an open benchmark at https://github.com/f-labs-io/tdad-paper-code.

摘要（中文）: 我们提出测试驱动的AI Agent定义（TDAD），这是一种将Agent prompt视为编译后的组件的方法：工程师提供行为规范，一个编码Agent将其转换为可执行的测试，而第二个编码Agent迭代地改进prompt，直到测试通过。在生产环境中部署使用工具的LLM Agent需要可衡量的行为合规性，而当前的开发实践无法提供这种保证。细微的prompt更改会导致静默回归，工具的错误使用无法被检测，策略违规仅在部署后才会显现。为了缓解规范博弈问题，TDAD引入了三种机制：（1）可见/隐藏测试拆分，在编译期间隐藏评估测试；（2）语义突变测试，通过编译后的Agent生成合理的错误prompt变体，并由测试框架衡量测试套件是否能检测到它们；以及（3）规范演化场景，用于量化需求变更时的回归安全性。我们在SpecSuite-Core上评估了TDAD，这是一个包含四个深度定制的Agent的基准测试，涵盖策略合规、基于事实的分析、运行手册遵循和确定性执行。在24次独立的试验中，TDAD的v1编译成功率达到92%，平均隐藏通过率为97%；演化的规范编译成功率为58%，大多数失败的运行都通过了除1-2个可见测试外的所有测试，并显示出86-100%的突变分数，78%的v2隐藏通过率和97%的回归安全分数。该实现已作为开放基准发布在https://github.com/f-labs-io/tdad-paper-code。

TLDR: The paper introduces Test-Driven AI Agent Definition (TDAD), a methodology for developing tool-using LLM agents with measurable behavioral compliance by using coding agents to create executable tests from behavioral specifications and iteratively refine prompts. TDAD includes mechanisms for mitigating specification gaming and quantifies regression safety.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种名为测试驱动的AI Agent定义(TDAD)的方法，通过使用编码Agent从行为规范创建可执行的测试，并迭代地改进提示，从而开发出具有可测量的行为合规性的工具使用LLM Agent。TDAD包括缓解规范博弈的机制，并量化了回归安全性。

Relevance: (7/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)
Overall: (8/10)
Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Tzafrir Rehan

VLM-Loc: Localization in Point Cloud Maps via Vision-Language Models

Abstract (English): Text-to-point-cloud (T2P) localization aims to infer precise spatial positions within 3D point cloud maps from natural language descriptions, reflecting how humans perceive and communicate spatial layouts through language. However, existing methods largely rely on shallow text-point cloud correspondence without effective spatial reasoning, limiting their accuracy in complex environments. To address this limitation, we propose VLM-Loc, a framework that leverages the spatial reasoning capability of large vision-language models (VLMs) for T2P localization. Specifically, we transform point clouds into bird's-eye-view (BEV) images and scene graphs that jointly encode geometric and semantic context, providing structured inputs for the VLM to learn cross-modal representations bridging linguistic and spatial semantics. On top of these representations, we introduce a partial node assignment mechanism that explicitly associates textual cues with scene graph nodes, enabling interpretable spatial reasoning for accurate localization. To facilitate systematic evaluation across diverse scenes, we present CityLoc, a benchmark built from multi-source point clouds for fine-grained T2P localization. Experiments on CityLoc demonstrate VLM-Loc achieves superior accuracy and robustness compared to state-of-the-art methods. Our code, model, and dataset are available at \href{https://github.com/MCG-NKU/nku-3d-vision}{repository}.

摘要（中文）: 文本到点云 (T2P) 定位旨在从自然语言描述中推断三维点云地图内的精确空间位置，反映了人类如何通过语言感知和交流空间布局。然而，现有方法主要依赖于浅层的文本-点云对应关系，缺乏有效的空间推理，限制了它们在复杂环境中的准确性。为了解决这一局限性，我们提出了 VLM-Loc，一个利用大型视觉-语言模型 (VLMs) 的空间推理能力进行 T2P 定位的框架。具体来说，我们将点云转换成鸟瞰图 (BEV) 图像和场景图，联合编码几何和语义上下文，为 VLM 提供结构化输入，以学习桥接语言和空间语义的跨模态表示。在这些表示的基础上，我们引入了一种部分节点分配机制，将文本线索与场景图节点显式关联，从而实现可解释的空间推理，以进行精确定位。为了促进跨各种场景的系统评估，我们提出了 CityLoc，一个由多源点云构建的用于细粒度 T2P 定位的基准。在 CityLoc 上的实验表明，与最先进的方法相比，VLM-Loc 实现了卓越的准确性和鲁棒性。我们的代码、模型和数据集可在 \href{https://github.com/MCG-NKU/nku-3d-vision}{repository} 获取。

TLDR: The paper introduces VLM-Loc, a novel framework leveraging VLMs for text-to-point-cloud localization by transforming point clouds into BEV images and scene graphs and using a partial node assignment mechanism for spatial reasoning, achieving state-of-the-art results on a new benchmark dataset, CityLoc.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了VLM-Loc，一种利用视觉语言模型进行文本到点云定位的新框架。它将点云转换为鸟瞰图和场景图，并使用部分节点分配机制进行空间推理，在新基准数据集 CityLoc 上实现了最先进的结果。

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Shuhao Kang, Youqi Liao, Peijie Wang, Wenlong Liao, Qilin Zhang, Benjamin Busam, Xieyuanli Chen, Yun Liu

X-GS: An Extensible Open Framework Unifying 3DGS Architectures with Downstream Multimodal Models

Abstract (English): 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has emerged as a powerful technique for novel view synthesis, subsequently extending into numerous spatial AI applications. However, most existing 3DGS methods are isolated, focusing on specific domains such as online SLAM, semantic enrichment, or 3DGS for unposed images. In this paper, we introduce X-GS, an extensible open framework that unifies a broad range of techniques to enable real-time 3DGS-based online SLAM enriched with semantics, bridging the gap to downstream multimodal models. At the core of X-GS is a highly efficient pipeline called X-GS-Perceiver, capable of taking unposed RGB (or optionally RGB-D) video streams as input to co-optimize geometry and poses, and distill high-dimensional semantic features from vision foundation models into the 3D Gaussians. We achieve real-time performance through a novel online Vector Quantization (VQ) module, a GPU-accelerated grid-sampling scheme, and a highly parallelized pipeline design. The semantic 3D Gaussians can then be utilized by vision-language models within the X-GS-Thinker component, enabling downstream tasks such as object detection, zero-shot caption generation, and potentially embodied tasks. Experimental results on real-world datasets showcase the efficacy, efficiency, and newly unlocked multimodal capabilities of the X-GS framework.

摘要（中文）: 3D高斯溅射(3DGS)已成为一种用于新视角合成的强大技术，并随后扩展到众多空间人工智能应用。然而，大多数现有的3DGS方法都是孤立的，侧重于特定领域，如在线SLAM、语义增强或用于无位姿图像的3DGS。在本文中，我们介绍X-GS，一个可扩展的开放框架，统一了广泛的技术，以实现基于3DGS的、具有语义增强的实时在线SLAM，从而弥合了与下游多模态模型的差距。X-GS的核心是一个高效的流水线，称为X-GS-Perceiver，它能够将无位姿的RGB（或可选的RGB-D）视频流作为输入，共同优化几何结构和位姿，并将来自视觉基础模型的高维语义特征提炼到3D高斯体中。我们通过一种新颖的在线向量量化(VQ)模块、一种GPU加速的网格采样方案以及高度并行化的流水线设计来实现实时性能。语义3D高斯体随后可被X-GS-Thinker组件中的视觉语言模型利用，从而实现诸如目标检测、零样本字幕生成以及潜在的具身任务等 downstream 任务。在真实世界数据集上的实验结果展示了 X-GS 框架的有效性、效率以及新解锁的多模态能力。

TLDR: The paper introduces X-GS, an extensible framework unifying 3D Gaussian Splatting architectures with multimodal models, enabling real-time online SLAM with semantic enrichment and downstream vision-language tasks.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了X-GS，一个可扩展的框架，统一了3D高斯溅射架构与多模态模型，实现了具有语义增强的实时在线SLAM以及下游的视觉-语言任务。

Relevance: (8/10)
Novelty: (7/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Read Paper (PDF)

Authors: Yueen Ma, Irwin King

More than the Sum: Panorama-Language Models for Adverse Omni-Scenes

Abstract (English): Existing vision-language models (VLMs) are tailored for pinhole imagery, stitching multiple narrow field-of-view inputs to piece together a complete omni-scene understanding. Yet, such multi-view perception overlooks the holistic spatial and contextual relationships that a single panorama inherently preserves. In this work, we introduce the Panorama-Language Modeling (PLM)paradigm, a unified $360^\circ$ vision-language reasoning that is more than the sum of its pinhole counterparts. Besides, we present PanoVQA, a large-scale panoramic VQA dataset that involves adverse omni-scenes, enabling comprehensive reasoning under object occlusions and driving accidents. To establish a foundation for PLM, we develop a plug-and-play panoramic sparse attention module that allows existing pinhole-based VLMs to process equirectangular panoramas without retraining. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our PLM achieves superior robustness and holistic reasoning under challenging omni-scenes, yielding understanding greater than the sum of its narrow parts. Project page: https://github.com/InSAI-Lab/PanoVQA.

摘要（中文）: 现有视觉-语言模型（VLMs）专为针孔图像设计，通过拼接多个窄视场输入以拼凑出完整的全景场景理解。然而，这种多视角感知忽略了单个全景图固有地保留的整体空间和上下文关系。在这项工作中，我们引入了全景-语言建模（PLM）范式，一种统一的$360^\circ$视觉-语言推理方法，它超越了针孔对应方法的总和。此外，我们提出了PanoVQA，一个大规模全景VQA数据集，其中包含各种不利的全景场景，从而能够在物体遮挡和驾驶事故等情况下进行全面的推理。为了为PLM奠定基础，我们开发了一个即插即用的全景稀疏注意力模块，该模块允许现有的基于针孔的VLM处理等距柱状投影全景图，而无需重新训练。大量实验表明，我们的PLM在具有挑战性的全景场景下实现了卓越的鲁棒性和整体推理能力，从而产生了大于其窄视场部分之和的理解。项目主页：https://github.com/InSAI-Lab/PanoVQA。

TLDR: The paper introduces a Panorama-Language Modeling (PLM) paradigm and a new dataset, PanoVQA, to enhance vision-language reasoning in omni-scenes using panoramic images. It also provides a plug-and-play module to adapt existing VLMs for panoramic data without retraining.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了一种全景语言建模（PLM）范式和一个新的数据集PanoVQA，旨在利用全景图像增强视觉语言在全向场景中的推理能力。它还提供了一个即插即用的模块，使现有的视觉语言模型能够处理全景数据而无需重新训练。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (9/10)
Potential Impact: (8/10)

Authors: Weijia Fan, Ruiping Liu, Jiale Wei, Yufan Chen, Junwei Zheng, Zichao Zeng, Jiaming Zhang, Qiufu Li, Linlin Shen, Rainer Stiefelhagen

GeoSolver: Scaling Test-Time Reasoning in Remote Sensing with Fine-Grained Process Supervision

Abstract (English): While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have significantly advanced remote sensing interpretation, enabling them to perform complex, step-by-step reasoning remains highly challenging. Recent efforts to introduce Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning to this domain have shown promise, yet ensuring the visual faithfulness of these intermediate steps remains a critical bottleneck. To address this, we introduce GeoSolver, a novel framework that transitions remote sensing reasoning toward verifiable, process-supervised reinforcement learning. We first construct Geo-PRM-2M, a large-scale, token-level process supervision dataset synthesized via entropy-guided Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) and targeted visual hallucination injection. Building upon this dataset, we train GeoPRM, a token-level process reward model (PRM) that provides granular faithfulness feedback. To effectively leverage these verification signals, we propose Process-Aware Tree-GRPO, a reinforcement learning algorithm that integrates tree-structured exploration with a faithfulness-weighted reward mechanism to precisely assign credit to intermediate steps. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our resulting model, GeoSolver-9B, achieves state-of-the-art performance across diverse remote sensing benchmarks. Crucially, GeoPRM unlocks robust Test-Time Scaling (TTS). Serving as a universal geospatial verifier, it seamlessly scales the performance of GeoSolver-9B and directly enhances general-purpose VLMs, highlighting its remarkable cross-model generalization.

摘要（中文）: 视觉-语言模型（VLMs）在遥感图像解译方面取得了显著进展，但使其执行复杂的、逐步推理仍然极具挑战性。 近期将思维链（CoT）推理引入该领域的工作展现了潜力，然而确保这些中间步骤的视觉忠实性仍然是一个关键瓶颈。 为了解决这个问题，我们引入了GeoSolver，一种新颖的框架，将遥感推理过渡到可验证的、过程监督强化学习。 我们首先构建了Geo-PRM-2M，一个大规模的、令牌级别的过程监督数据集，该数据集通过熵引导的蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）和有针对性的视觉幻觉注入合成。 在此数据集的基础上，我们训练了GeoPRM，一个令牌级别的过程奖励模型（PRM），它提供细粒度的忠实性反馈。 为了有效利用这些验证信号，我们提出了一种过程感知树-GRPO的强化学习算法，该算法将树状结构的探索与忠实性加权的奖励机制相结合，以精确地将功劳分配给中间步骤。 大量的实验表明，我们最终的模型GeoSolver-9B在各种遥感基准测试中都达到了最先进的性能。 关键的是，GeoPRM解锁了稳健的测试时缩放（TTS）。 作为一个通用的地理空间验证器，它无缝地扩展了GeoSolver-9B的性能，并直接增强了通用VLM，突出了其卓越的跨模型泛化能力。

TLDR: The paper introduces GeoSolver, a framework for improving the reasoning capabilities of VLMs in remote sensing by using process supervision and reinforcement learning with a novel reward model that ensures visual faithfulness of intermediate steps, leading to state-of-the-art performance and test-time scaling.

简要说明: 该论文介绍了 GeoSolver，一个通过过程监督和强化学习来提高 VLMs 在遥感领域推理能力的工作，该方法利用了一种新的奖励模型来确保中间步骤的可视化保真度，从而实现最先进的性能和测试时扩展。

Relevance: (9/10)
Novelty: (8/10)
Clarity: (8/10)
